issue: The kind discriminator crosses the whole API as an untyped 'string kind'. Per-kind endpoint files pass bare literals ('book','collection','audio-track','audio-library') that duplicate the EntityKind [Code] values, and EntityKindRouteEndpoints/EntityDetailEndpoint forward the raw URL {kind} string straight into IEntityReadService/IEntityMetadataPatchService with no conversion to EntityKind via EnumCodec<EntityKind>. This is the canonical-code system being bypassed at the API boundary: a typo in any Map call silently routes to a non-existent kind, and there is no compile-time link back to EntityKind. Strongly recommend the Map* helpers accept EntityKind and resolve the code through EnumCodec, and the route handlers decode {kind} once at the edge.

ToProblem maps FileOperationException.Code by comparing raw strings ("root_not_found"/"not_found") that are defined and thrown in the Application layer (FilesService). The string contract is duplicated across the Api/Application boundary with no shared constant, so renaming a code in FilesService silently breaks the 404 mapping here. Centralize the file-operation problem codes in one shared definition.

MapEntityKindRoutes accepts the entity kind as a raw `string kind` parameter, which forces every caller (MovieEndpoints, MusicArtistEndpoints, and ~12 other endpoint files using this helper) to pass a bare string literal that must exactly match an EntityKind [Code]. This is the structural root cause of the magic-string entity-kind drift across the whole Api endpoint layer: a typo or rename silently desyncs the route from the canonical code with no compile-time check. The helper should take the typed `EntityKind` (or a code resolved from it) so callers reference the enum and the literal lives only at the [Code] declaration.

The shared MapEntityKindRoutes helper takes the entity kind as a raw `string kind` parameter, which forces every caller (PeopleEndpoints, StudioEndpoints, TagEndpoints, VideoCatalogEndpoints, SeriesEndpoints) to hand-type the [Code] string. Changing the helper signature to accept `EntityKind` (and calling EntityKindRegistry.ToCode internally) would eliminate all six bare-literal call sites at once and make the canonical enum the single source of truth. .ToCode()/EntityKindRegistry is already used elsewhere in the Api (IdentifyBulkEndpoints.cs, EntityListEndpoint.cs).

UpdateCheckResponse status ("available"/"current"/"unknown"/"development") and release channel ("dev") are emitted as bare string literals with no shared contract; the Svelte client (ChangelogDialog.svelte:130-131) branches on the exact same literals independently. This is a closed-set wire vocabulary that should flow through a [Code] enum / codes.ts the way EntityKind/JobType do, otherwise the two ends can drift silently. Also GhcrUpdateCheckService is a non-trivial networking/version-comparison service living inside an Api endpoint file rather than Application/Infrastructure.

Entity-kind string dispatch is the dominant pattern across the whole JellyfinCatalogService partial (IsPlayable, IsAudio, IsFolder, JellyfinType, CollectionType, and dozens of inline `kind.Equals("...")` checks in .cs/.Mapping.cs). These hardcode the [Code] strings of EntityKind instead of decoding to the enum via EnumCodec/CodecRegistry. Any rename of an entity-kind code silently breaks the Jellyfin projection with no compile error. Should decode IEntityCard.Kind/EntityThumbnail.Kind to EntityKind once and switch on the enum.

Codec/container/runtime/audio-codec for thumbnails are reverse-engineered by string-matching display chips in EntityThumbnail.Meta (Icon=="video"/"audio"/"duration", then parsing the human Label e.g. splitting a duration string, excluding labels containing "p" to guess container vs resolution). This couples the Jellyfin wire projection to UI presentation text — a layering inversion: technical facts should come from a structured projection, not from parsing rendered chip labels. Fragile (e.g. a codec whose name contains 'p' breaks container detection at lines 158-159, 206).

Files problem codes (file_conflict, invalid_path, root_not_found, not_found) are bare string literals duplicated across the producer (FilesService/FileConflictException) and the consumer endpoint mapping (FilesEndpoints.cs:83 matches the same literals with ==). No shared constants class — a rename on one side silently breaks HTTP status mapping. Extract a FilesProblemCodes constants class referenced by both.

Cache layout (per-kind subdir names 'videos'/'images'/'book-pages'/'audio-tracks' AND the per-entity asset file paths) is re-derived here independently of Infrastructure's AssetPathService, which is the component that actually writes those paths. GetExpectedAssetPaths() and CleanOrphanedCacheDirs() reconstruct the same path scheme by hand, so any change to the cache layout in AssetPathService silently breaks maintenance (false 'missing asset' counts or failure to find orphan dirs). Maintenance should ask the asset path service for the directory/paths rather than hardcode them. The same subdir strings are triplicated in MaintenancePersistenceService.cs.

BuildStreams synthesizes MediaStreamProbeData with hardcoded Type="Video"/"Audio" strings that are then dispatched on by string equality in many Infrastructure/Application video services. The producer here and ~10 consumers share an undeclared stream-type vocabulary with no single constant, so a casing/spelling change in one place silently breaks playback selection. Define a shared stream-type constant set and use it on both sides.

Two-sided drift hazard: the selector-kind vocabulary lives both as the *values* of the AutoIdentifySelectorKinds dictionary and as the default list of the autoIdentify.entityKinds setting, compared case-insensitively in AutoIdentifyScanEnqueue.cs. Adding a new media kind requires editing three disconnected places (the map, the setting default, and the entity-kind->selector mapping) with no compile-time link. Should be a single shared constant set consumed by all three.

The patch-field protocol (Fields list of selector keys) is an untyped string contract with the closed set living only in a doc comment. Its consumers — EntityMetadataApplyService.cs, EntityMetadataPatchValidator.cs, AutoIdentifyRunner.cs — independently hardcode the same literals (title/description/urls/dates/rating/flags) and could silently drift (e.g. a typo in one place would be a no-op patch). This is the strongest case in the cluster for a shared constants/enum home.

Position codes are modeled as a raw string Code on Item with no closed-set type, while sibling domain concepts (BookType, BookFormat, ReaderMode, EntityFileRole, EntityKind) all use [Code] enums + EnumCodec. This is an inconsistency in the very layer that owns the codec gold standard: position is a closed structural vocabulary (season/volume/chapter/page/track/sort/episode) yet skips the codec, so the literal vocabulary is re-typed across Infrastructure plugin rules, scan persistence, and the Jellyfin mapping with no compile-time guard against drift.

Same pattern as CapabilityPosition: EntityDate.Code and EntityDate.Precision are closed vocabularies (release/air/birth/career-start; year/month/day/text) modeled as free-form strings instead of [Code] enums. The Jellyfin DatePriority array (JellyfinCatalogService.cs) and several persistence/StashCompat writers each hardcode these strings independently, so a typo on either side silently drops dates.

Hand-rolled SQL string builder for a user-driven rule tree. Although values are parameterized via NpgsqlParameter (so not directly injectable), column names, table names, JOIN clauses, and the entity-kind/relationship/role/date codes are all string-interpolated raw SQL that bypasses the EF model entirely. This duplicates schema knowledge (column/table names, entity_dates codes, role='source') that lives in EF mappings, and the whole field/operator vocabulary is an un-shared contract with the frontend rule builder. High drift risk: a column rename or code change in the canonical enums will silently break rule evaluation with no compile-time link.

Raw SQL filters on `e.deleted_at IS NULL` (BuildSql:65, TranslateChildCount:340) but the rest of the codebase (per project memory 'Hard delete only — no soft delete/DeletedAt') does hard deletes; if the entities table has no deleted_at column this query would error, and if it does it is an abandoned soft-delete remnant. Either way it contradicts the hard-delete contract and the EF read services in this same cluster never filter deleted_at.

Status/sort vocabularies (ParseSort aliases, ApplyListFilters status keys) and the collection rule field/operator vocabularies are server-only string sets that the frontend must produce identically. None are sourced from a [Code] enum or codes.ts, so they sit outside the canonical identifier system this codebase otherwise enforces — the single largest body of un-anchored wire vocabulary in the cluster.

Uses the bare literal "tags" for the relationship-code filter (line 77) while every other code reference in the same file goes through EntityKindRegistry.*.Code / EntityFileRole enums — an inconsistency that defeats the canonical codec for exactly one query and will drift if RelationshipKind.Tags's code ever changes.

Credit-link MetadataJson is hand-built with a raw interpolated string literal {"role":..,"roles":[..]} while the canonical write path (CreditsCapabilityMapper.cs:98) serializes a typed CreditMetadata record. Two independent shapes for the same DB column: drift risk, and the scan path emits an extra "roles" array the typed record does not. Worse, the value "performer" is not a CreditRole code (canonical actor code is "actor"), and the Jellyfin read projection filters Person.Type on "actor" (JellyfinCatalogService.Metadata.cs:188) — scanned performers likely will not be projected as actors. Should reuse the typed serializer and a CreditRole code.

Position codes ("episode", "absolute-episode", "season") and date codes ("release") are bare string literals with no canonical [Code] enum, yet the same literals are read back on the projection side (JellyfinCatalogService.Mapping.cs:185-186,336). This is exactly the closed-set dispatch-key drift the EnumCodec/[Code] system is meant to eliminate; an EntityPositionCode/EntityDateCode enum with [Code] should own these so writer and reader reference one source.

SelectAllPresentFields hardcodes the patch->field-key mapping that is conceptually owned by the apply pipeline. The set of selectable fields and their literal keys is duplicated between this producer and the EntityMetadataApplyService/Validator consumers; adding a new patch field requires editing both sides in sync with no compiler enforcement. Extract a single field-key vocabulary (with a patch-presence helper) shared by producer and consumers.

Position-code knowledge is split: NormalizeCode/SortOrderFor own the canonical mapping in EntityMetadataPositionRules, but EnsureStructuralPositions (StructuralProposals.cs:383) re-derives the raw wire keys ('seasonNumber'/'sortOrder') independently and ResolveStructuralPositionsAsync (:507,525) seeds them. The two files must stay in lockstep with no shared constant — a drift here silently breaks sort ordering.

Two overloads of IsSameStructuralChild exist (proposal-vs-proposal at :246 and StructuralChild-vs-proposal at :353) plus AreCompatibleProposalKinds (:268) and IsCompatibleStructuralKind (:372) — four near-identical kind-equality/sort-order matchers encoding the same 'video == video-episode' rule. This is duplicated matching logic that should collapse into one structural-identity comparer.

ClaimNextAsync embeds its own inline FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED SQL with hardcoded 'running'/'queued' literals, duplicating JobQueueSql.ClaimNext which expresses the same query via JobRunStatus.ToCode(). Two divergent copies of the claim statement: the service ignores JobQueueSql entirely and the literals can drift from the enum codes.

JobQueueSql (ClaimNext/MarkCompleted/MarkFailed) appears to be dead/parallel code: JobQueueService implements claim/complete/fail with its own inline SQL and EF mutations and never references JobQueueSql. Either wire the service to these strings or remove them to avoid two sources of truth for the same statements.

Credit role 'performer' does not exist in the canonical CreditRole enum (which has 'actor' and 'person' but no 'performer'). Either the apply pipeline silently coerces unknown roles or this is a latent correctness bug where scene cast is stored under an unrecognized role code. Should map performers to CreditRole.Actor (or Person) via EnumCodec.

'video-episode' is compared as an entity-kind literal but no EntityKind member declares [Code("video-episode")] (the enum models series/season, not episode). This kind gate may never match the intended kind or relies on a code defined elsewhere; verify against EntityKindRegistry to avoid a dead branch.

The 'stash-compat' runtime code is declared independently in two places (StashCompatRunner.RuntimeCode and StashScraperManifestFactory.Runtime). A manifest produced with one copy is matched by CanRun using the other; they must stay byte-identical but nothing enforces it. Extract one shared constant.

Image-asset kinds ('thumbnail'/'logo'/'poster') and entity kinds ('person'/'studio') are emitted as bare strings here while the consumer (EntityMetadataApplyService.Relationships) also hardcodes the same literals. Both sides should reference EntityFileRole / EntityKind EnumCodec codes so the producer/consumer contract is type-checked rather than string-coupled.

meta.icon string-matching is a fragile cross-boundary contract: the FE searches EntityThumbnail.meta for icon === "duration"/"video"/"image" while the backend emits those same literals in JellyfinCatalogService.MediaStreams.cs and the thumbnail contributors. EntityThumbnailMeta.icon is typed only as `string`, so any backend rename silently breaks card duration/resolution with no compile error. Should be a shared closed-set code (generated codes.ts) rather than free-form strings on both sides.

Whole file is a hand-maintained parallel type model that duplicates generated OpenAPI contracts. CLAUDE.md says the frontend should prefer generated types and .NET contracts are the source of truth. PluginProvider, EntityMetadataProposal/Patch, IdentifyQueueItem, EntitySearchCandidate, IdentifyApplyProgress etc. are redeclared here and then cast onto generated request/response types in identify-client.ts (e.g. 'as ApplyIdentifyQueueItemRequest', 'as IdentifyQueueItem'), so server shape drift will not be caught by the compiler.

Massive duplication: prismedia.ts re-implements wrappers that already live in the focused per-feature files in the same dir. createJob/cancelJobs/cancelJobRun/clearJobFailures/rebuildPreviews/backfillFingerprints/fetchWorkerHealth/fetchJobs duplicate jobs.ts; fetchSettings/fetchSetting/updateSetting/updateSettings/resetSetting/fetchSettingsValues/normalize* duplicate settings.ts; updateEntityPlayback/updateEntityProgress/Jellyfin playback fns duplicate playback.ts; all media fetchers duplicate media.ts. Two diverging implementations of the same surface is a real drift/correctness hazard (see next two notes).

Divergent behavior vs jobs.ts for the same operations. prismedia.fetchWorkerHealth calls fetchApi("/health/worker") and does NOT run normalizeWorkerHealth, so staleAfterSeconds stays a string when the API returns a string; jobs.ts coerces it to a number. Likewise prismedia.cancelJobs/clearJobs/rebuildPreviews/backfillFingerprints skip the normalizeNumber step that jobs.ts applies, and prismedia.fetchJobs/fetchVideos/etc. return response.data WITHOUT unwrapGenerated error handling (no thrown error on non-2xx) whereas media.ts/jobs.ts unwrap. Callers get different types/error semantics depending on which module they import.

prismedia.updateEntityProgress posts a different payload than playback.updateEntityProgress: it omits reset and location fields (and hits raw fetchApi(/entities/{id}/progress) instead of the generated updateEntityProgressRequest). playback.ts sends reset:false and location:null. Two progress writers with different request bodies for the same endpoint is a latent correctness bug.

LibrarySettings is duplicated almost verbatim in apps/web-svelte/src/lib/api/prismedia.ts:207-234, and the two copies have already drifted: the prismedia.ts copy is missing the autoIdentify* fields (autoIdentifyEnabled/Providers/EntityKinds/ConfidenceThreshold/UnorganizedOnly) that settings.ts defines. Two parallel hand-written API modules (settings.ts vs prismedia.ts) declaring the same domain shape is a layering/duplication hazard; consolidate to one source.

Entity-kind taxonomy is forked: this module redeclares the eligible collection entity kinds as a local CollectionEntityType union + COLLECTION_ENTITY_TYPES array + inline entityTypes literals in COLLECTION_RULE_FIELDS, parallel to the canonical ENTITY_KIND generated codes (entity-codes.ts / codes.ts). The comment on line 9 says it mirrors backend Collection.CanContain, but nothing enforces that mirror — if the backend rule or the generated codes change, these three copies drift silently. Should be derived from ENTITY_KIND.

DeletableEntity is a parallel, hand-maintained entity-kind taxonomy divorced from ENTITY_KIND. It introduces "series" which does NOT equal ENTITY_KIND.videoSeries ("video-series"), so callers must translate, and the singular/plural label map duplicates label data that the generated ENTITY_KIND_LABELS already owns. Drift risk: adding a deletable kind requires editing this union + map instead of the central codes.

isActiveQueueState hardcodes terminal states ("done"/"deleted") instead of referencing the generated IDENTIFY_QUEUE_STATE const, and computes 'active' as a negation of two literals; if a new terminal state is added to the backend enum the button will wrongly treat it as active. Should derive from the codegen enum (e.g. an ACTIVE/terminal set).

SearchEntityKind is a parallel entity-kind vocabulary that overlaps ENTITY_KIND (generated from backend [Code] enums) but diverges: it uses 'performer' where the canonical code is 'person', and omits codes like book-volume/season. SearchResultCard branches on these literals throughout. This is a second source of truth for entity kinds and a drift risk; the search models should derive from / map to ENTITY_KIND rather than redefine kinds.

Upload/extract/delete/save track handlers are all stubbed to set cuesError = 'Subtitle editing is waiting on the API.' — the entire track-management UI (inputs, buttons, edit form) is wired to no-op mutations. Dead/placeholder write path shipped as interactive controls; either gate behind a flag or finish the API wiring.

CollectionEntityType is a hand-maintained re-typing of a subset of the canonical ENTITY_KIND codes. It (and every consumer) drifts independently of the generated codes.ts / backend [Code] enums — the gold-standard identifier system is bypassed for the entire collections slice. Should be a derived subset of EntityKindCode so adding/renaming a kind backend-side flows through.

getEntityHref re-implements kind->route resolution with a local switch and inline slug literals, duplicating resolveEntityHref() in $lib/entities/entity-codes.ts (which already centralizes ROUTE_RULES). Two sources of truth for routing means a route change must be made in both places; collection links can silently diverge from the rest of the app.

Filter-id vocabulary is duplicated across a producer/parser boundary. The drawer emits id strings (status:*, flags:*, files:*, taxonomy:*, rating:min/max, dates:from/to, technical:resolution/duration/codec) and entity-grid.ts independently re-hardcodes the identical strings to parse them (entityGridFilterFromId at ~513-571, buildCapabilityFilterOptions). Resolution/codec/duration value vocabularies are likewise duplicated. Any new filter or renamed bucket must be edited in two places or it silently no-ops. Centralize id builders + parsers in one module the drawer imports.

presetSorts allowlist (line 622) hardcodes the sort keys and omits "references" that the canonical EntityGridSort union (entity-grid.ts:34) includes, so a saved preset sorted by references silently falls back to initialSortBy. Derive the runtime list from the union / a single ENTITY_GRID_SORTS const.

Single 2724-line component owns hero, asset upload pipeline, rating, flags, tabbed+standalone metadata, and the entire inline edit form. The section dispatch is a 50+ branch switch (renderDetailSection) duplicating the id set also enumerated in coreSections, sectionEditable, and sectionHasDisplayContent; edit snippets are near-duplicated KeyValueEditor wrappers. Maintenance and correctness risk is high — adding a section requires touching 4 parallel lists. Split into hero / metadata-sections / edit-form subcomponents driven by a shared section registry.

The 'directory'/'file' file-system entry kind has no shared frontend constant (FileEntry.kind is typed as bare string in the generated model), so the literal 'directory' is duplicated across FileDetailPane, FileTreePane, file-tree-state, and file-actions with drift risk. A single FILE_ENTRY_KIND const (ideally backed by a backend [Code] enum) would close the gap.

entityKindLabel duplicates the canonical ENTITY_KIND_LABELS / labelForEntityKind table from $lib/entities/entity-codes, but with a DIFFERENT, substring-based mapping (e.g. it returns People/Studios/Tags/Seasons/Episodes via .includes checks). This is parallel label logic that will drift from the generated label source of truth.

isRelationshipKind() and IDENTIFY_CONTAINER_KINDS re-derive backend domain knowledge on the frontend with hardcoded entity-kind strings. The comment on IDENTIFY_CONTAINER_KINDS explicitly says it 'Mirrors the backend EntityKindRegistry.EnumeratesIdentifyChildren flag' — a classic drift hazard. These should reference ENTITY_KIND.* constants and ideally be fed by a generated registry flag rather than a copied list.

Heavy duplication: the base-field diff table, credits/relationships sections, tag chips, and the artwork-selection grid (markup AND the ~140-line <style> block defining identify-artwork-grid/identify-artwork-tile/shimmer keyframes) are copy-pasted between the two files with only minor differences (Parent adds thumbnail/still artwork kinds + apply-progress + queue nav). Selection-state effects and setRelationshipSelected/setTagSelected/setFieldSelected/setAllFields/setImageSelected are byte-for-byte identical. This should be factored into shared snippets/components and a shared CSS module to prevent drift.

Local entityKindLabel() reimplements the canonical ENTITY_KIND_LABELS/labelForEntityKind and DRIFTS from it (e.g. 'Albums' vs canonical 'Audio Libraries', and omits the 'audio' kind). This is exactly the drift the generated codes layer exists to prevent — the supportedKinds tab labels can diverge from the rest of the app.

relationshipGroupMatchesKind hardcodes both the entity-kind targets and the relationship-group aliases (cast/credits/tags/studios) as lowercased bare strings, doing string normalization instead of comparing against RELATIONSHIP_CODE / ENTITY_KIND. This is fragile mapping logic that should reference the canonical codes the backend already emits.

The SEARCH_KIND_CONFIG / ALL_SEARCH_KINDS data and the SearchEntityKind union (search/models.ts) hand-maintain the entity-kind vocabulary (movie, video-series, performer, audio-track, ...) which exactly duplicates the canonical generated ENTITY_KIND codes in api/generated/codes.ts. This is a second source of truth for entity kinds that can drift from the backend [Code] enums; SearchEntityKind/config should be derived from or keyed by ENTITY_KIND rather than redeclared. (The test is in this cluster; the duplication lives in the config/type it covers.)

Security-sensitive HTML sanitization is implemented with hand-rolled regexes (UNSAFE_BLOCK_RE/EVENT_HANDLER_RE/UNSAFE_URL_RE/UNSAFE_SCHEME_RE) rather than a real sanitizer (DOMPurify). Regex HTML sanitization is a known-fragile pattern: it misses malformed/unclosed tags, nested/encoded payloads, SVG/mathml vectors, and attribute-splitting tricks. The tests cover only a handful of vectors. For user-editable descriptions this is a real XSS surface.

buildMetadataUpdate hardcodes credit role as "performer" (line 224) which is not a member of the backend CREDIT_ROLE enum (person/actor/director/...). Credits saved through the edit form will round-trip with a role code the server enum does not define, a likely correctness/data-integrity bug.

Filter-id vocabulary is duplicated across four functions in one file with no shared catalog: isServerResolvedFilterId (eq/startsWith checks), buildServerQueryFromFilters (parse), buildCapabilityFilterOptions (build), entityGridFilterFromId (re-resolve), and entityMatchesFilter (client match) each independently spell out the same prefixes (flags:, rating:min:, technical:resolution:, book-type:, status:, etc.). Adding or renaming a filter requires editing 4-5 sites with no compile-time link; the test file spells them a 5th time. Should extract a single filter-id builder/parser (e.g. FILTER family enum + encode/decode) so the id grammar has one owner.

VALID_SORTS / VALID_SORT_DIRS / VALID_VIEW_MODES are runtime arrays that re-list every member of the EntityGridSort / EntityGridSortDir / EntityGridViewMode union types defined in entity-grid.ts. The type and the validator can drift independently (add a sort to the type, forget the array, and persisted values silently fall back). entity-grid.ts should export the canonical runtime tuples and derive the types from them (as const + typeof), and prefs should import those tuples.

Duplicates routing knowledge that already lives in entity-codes.ts ROUTE_RULES: it hand-builds /books/${id}/volumes/${id} and /books/${id}/chapters/${id} URL strings instead of calling resolveEntityHref(kind, id, parentContext). Two sources of truth for the same book/series URL shapes; a route change in ROUTE_RULES would not propagate here. The bookChapter branch also encodes the volume->book grandparent walk that the canonical resolver could express via parent context.

Job-type and queue metadata is duplicated across FOUR independent tables that must be kept in sync by hand. _JOB_DEFINITIONS (jobs-dashboard.ts) maps type->queue/label/desc, queueDefinitions (models.ts) re-declares queue labels/descriptions, RUN_CATALOG (run-catalog.ts) re-declares jobType+queueName+label+icon, and queueIcons (helpers.ts) re-maps queue->icon. The same labels/descriptions are written in 2-3 places (e.g. 'Video Scan' vs 'Library Scan', and library-maintenance description appears verbatim in both _JOB_DEFINITIONS and queueDefinitions). All of these should derive from a single job/queue registry keyed by the canonical JOB_TYPE codes.

_JOB_DEFINITIONS is missing many job types that exist in the canonical JOB_TYPE / backend JobType enum: generate-grid-thumbnail, generate-book-cover-thumbnail, refresh-entity, identify-cascade. Any API JobRun with one of those types falls through definitionForJob's default branch and is mislabeled (raw type string as label) and forced into the 'library-maintenance' queue, corrupting queue summaries. Driving definitions from the generated JOB_TYPE codes would make this gap impossible.

Correctness/coupling: hidden-entity detection relies on string-matching the backend error message and problem code from the frontend. Because the backend emits 'entity_not_found' as a bare literal in many endpoints with no shared constant, any rename or message-wording change silently breaks the SFW redirect with no compile-time signal. This is the same root duplication as the magic-string finding but is an architecture-level fragility (no typed contract across the API boundary for problem codes).

Vocabulary drift: the local subtitleDisplayStyles is {stylized, classic, outline} while the generated/backend SUBTITLE_STYLE is {stylized, plain}. The two diverge on every member except 'stylized', so the player's style codes and the server's settings-key codes describe different sets. One is stale or the codegen source is out of sync — reconcile to a single canonical SubtitleDisplayStyle.

SubtitleSource is hand-declared as a TS union that exactly duplicates the generated SUBTITLE_SOURCE const (which is itself generated from backend EntitySubtitleSource [Code]). The canonical generated const exists but is not used here, so adding/removing a backend subtitle source will silently not propagate to this type.

VideoPlaybackMode ('direct'|'hls') is declared locally and threaded through every function/interface here, duplicating the backend-generated PLAYBACK_MODE/PlaybackModeCode in src/lib/api/generated/codes.ts. Since these are wire/route-affecting values (effectiveMode is also embedded in loadKey and drives URL selection), the player should consume the generated codes/type so it cannot drift from the server vocabulary.

Near-total duplication. SeriesListPrefs/VideosListPrefs share the same shape (viewMode/sortBy/sortDir/search/activeFilters/activePresetId), and the helpers parseActiveFilters (series 65-83 vs videos 59-77 are byte-for-byte identical), defaultXListPrefs, isDefaultXListPrefs (identical JSON.stringify compare), validateXListPrefs, the ActiveFilter interface, and the *FilterValue formatter are copy-pasted with only the sort-option list and a couple of filter types differing. This should be a single generic list-prefs builder (parameterized by sort options + filter-type table), mirroring the existing createListPrefs factory in $lib/list-prefs.ts which already abstracts the cookie variant. The filter-type vocabulary and the to-fetch-params translation are the main per-entity differences and could be data-driven.

SearchEntityKind is a hand-maintained parallel entity-kind vocabulary that shadows the auto-generated EntityKindCode. It both omits canonical kinds (book-volume/chapter/page, video-season, music-artist, audio) and renames 'person' to 'performer', so the search layer has its own kind taxonomy that can silently drift from the backend [Code] source of truth. Should be derived from EntityKindCode with an explicit, single-sourced performer<->person alias.

Local playbackModes tuple + PlaybackMode type + normalizePlaybackMode duplicate the generated PLAYBACK_MODE const (codes.ts) and the PlaybackModeCode type. This is a parallel, hand-maintained copy of a closed set the codegen already owns; drift between the two would silently break playback-mode dispatch. Should be derived from the generated codes instead of redefined.

tag/studio/person detail pages are near-identical copies: same LoadState type, nsfw effect, breadcrumb effect, load/loadRelated/rating/favorite/organized/metadata handlers, error-notice markup, and scoped CSS — only the API call (getTag/getStudio/getPerson), relationshipCode, and labels differ. This is ~120 lines duplicated 3x (4x with movie). A generic SimpleEntityDetailPage component parameterized by fetcher + relationshipCode + copy would collapse them.

SearchEntityKind is a hand-maintained frontend union ('movie','performer','video-series','audio-library'...) that diverges from the backend-generated ENTITY_KIND ('person','videoSeries','audioLibrary'). The search page, search-kind-config, and entity-search all branch on this parallel vocabulary, creating a second source of truth for entity kinds that can drift from the generated codes. The mapping from SearchEntityKind to backend kinds is implicit.

loadChapterSummaries is duplicated almost verbatim (the full volume-walk / cross-volume next-chapter stitching, ~50 lines) between the reader route and the chapter detail route. This non-trivial reading-order logic should live once in $lib/entities/book-entity-reader.ts (alongside orderedBookChildren) and be imported by both; divergence here would silently break next-chapter navigation in one place only.

positionForReaderIndex (map a flat reader index back to {chapter, pageIndex, pageCount}) is reimplemented in both the reader route and the volume route with the same algorithm but different shapes (reader uses chapter.detail.id, volume uses chapter.id). This page-offset math is reading-position correctness logic and should be a single shared helper to avoid off-by-one drift between the two.

Book reading-model branching keys off untyped string fields (book.format / book.bookType are `string` on the generated BookDetail). Format/type are closed sets used for dispatch (epub/pdf/image-archive; comic/manga) yet there is no [Code]-backed enum or generated const for them — the only enumeration lives as inline objects in entity-grid.ts. This is the canonical-codes gap most worth closing for the book domain: promote book-type and book-format to [Code] enums so the wire field is typed and routes reference constants.

Two distinct rating-update paths coexist for lightbox images: handleLightboxRatingChange (line 223, called via UniversalLightbox onRatingChange) calls updateLightboxCardRating then persists via updateEntityRating, while the ImageLightboxDetails snippet (line 366) passes onRatingChange={updateLightboxCardRating} which only mutates local state and does NOT persist. Depending on which control the user uses, a rating change may or may not be saved to the server - a likely correctness/consistency bug.

The community tab inlines a full duplicate of the PluginCredentialForm (per-field password input, link button, canSave guard, Cancel/Save). The reusable form already exists and is imported by InstalledPluginsTab; PrismediaCommunityTab should compose it. The duplicate also re-encodes the `prismedia:${plugin.id}` saving-key convention, doubling the drift surface.

loadInstalled() always sets installedPlugins = [] (line 97) and there is no other assignment, so the installed-plugins list is dead state. The InstalledPluginsTab branch for installedPlugins, the visibleInstalledPlugins filter, handlePluginToggle/Remove/SaveAuth, and pluginUpdates map are effectively unreachable through the current load path. Either the load is incomplete (bug) or a large block of plugin-handling code is dead and should be removed.

PluginsTab union declares "stashbox" but no tab definition, icon case, render branch, or load path uses it (visibleTabs only lists installed/prismedia-index/stash-index). StashBoxEndpointsTab.svelte is a fully-built component with tests but is never imported or rendered by +page.svelte — orphaned feature / dead route surface.

This file is a near-line-for-line clone of [id]/+page.svelte: identical LoadState type, nsfw/appChrome wiring, ratingBusy + handleRatingChange/Favorite/Organized/MetadataSave handlers, detailTabs/detailSections derivation, loading/error/retry scaffold, and CSS block. The only real differences are which entity is loaded and the heroBadges/season-number logic. Strong candidate to factor the shared detail-page mechanics into a composable (e.g. useEntityDetailPage) so the two routes stop drifting independently.

Jellyfin session-progress event scalars ('Playing', 'Playing/Progress', 'Playing/Stopped') and the magic PositionTicks factor 10_000_000 are hardcoded inline across four call sites. These are wire-protocol constants that should be centralized (a shared progress-event const + a seconds->ticks helper) so the page can't drift from the backend JellyfinProtocol vocab. The union type already lives in playback.ts:137 but is not exported as values.

Dead/legacy persistence vocabulary: per CLAUDE.md the .NET backend is the source of truth and the frontend should prefer generated OpenAPI types + api/generated/codes.ts. This package still hand-maintains DTOs (frontend-dtos.ts), queue/job/status vocab (jobs.ts), route templates (routes.ts), playback/subtitle codes, and an EntityKind union that all duplicate the generated backend contracts. It is consumed by ~30 frontend/plugin files. This is a parallel, drift-prone contract layer that the codegen system was meant to replace.

queueDefinitions/jobStatuses/jobTriggerKinds/jobKinds and the concurrency helpers describe a BullMQ worker model ('BullMQ jobs per queue', resolveQueueWorkerConcurrency). The system is now a .NET worker (CLAUDE.md: 'Never introduce ... a TypeScript worker'; JobType lives in the .NET domain). This queue vocabulary is largely orphaned relative to the canonical JobType [Code] set and should be reconciled or removed.

Concrete divergence of the duplicated layout above: VIDEO_GENERATED_FILENAMES.thumb = "thumbnail.jpg" for the dedicated layout, while the backend AssetPathService.cs:19 uses "thumb.jpg" for the same videos/<id>/ directory. The TS dedicated-thumbnail path does not match what the backend writes/reads, a latent correctness bug if any TS code resolves dedicated-layout thumbnails.

runNativePythonPlugin and runNativePythonPluginBatch are ~95% duplicated: identical script-empty guard, PYTHONPATH env construction, spawn/stdio setup, timeout+SIGTERM, error/close handlers, exit-code 0/69 check, and 'null' sentinel parsing. Only the envelope (input vs batch), default timeout (30s vs 60s), and the result vs results extraction differ. Should be unified into one parametrized runner (or a shared spawn-and-collect helper) to avoid divergence.

Heavy duplication of normalizer scaffolding: normalizeVideoResult/Gallery/Book/Image/AudioTrack/AudioLibrary all repeat the same trimOrNull/toUrlArray/toStringArray/trimToUrl/alias-fallback patterns inline. Additionally this file and normalized-video.ts independently re-implement near-identical helpers (isObject/toStringOrNull/toNumberOrNull/toExternalIds/isValidUrl vs trimOrNull/toNumber/toExternalIds/trimToUrl) with subtly different behavior (e.g. integer parsing: normalizer.ts uses parseInt for width/height, normalized-video.ts uses parseFloat+trunc), and different default image source ('plugin' vs 'unknown'). These two normalizer modules should share one helper layer.

deduplicateUrls is declared but the data-URL keying logic (slice 100) duplicates intent of trimToUrl/validation; more notably normalizeSceneResult applies HTML-entity decoding + url-scheme validation + date coercion, while the parallel stashbox/normalizer.ts:17 does NONE of this (it passes scene.title/details/url/imageUrl straight through with only `?? null`). Two normalizers feeding the same NormalizedScrapeResult shape apply inconsistent sanitization — StashBox results skip trimming, entity-decode, and the http(s)/data-image URL allowlist that the community path enforces.

ApiProblem error codes are bare string literals scattered across every endpoint file with no central catalog. 'entity_not_found' alone is duplicated across at least EntityEndpointResults, EntityDetailEndpoint, EntityImageAssetEndpoint and EntityKindRouteEndpoints; 'collection_not_found' appears 5x in one file. These are machine-readable problem codes (the same category the prompt calls problem-code) and should live in a shared ApiProblemCodes constant set to prevent drift between server emission and any client/test that branches on them.

Archive-entry addressing uses a magic '::' separator convention (path.Split("::")) to encode archivePath::memberPath inside a single string. This couples the API layer to a path-encoding convention that must match whoever produces the file Path (the read/scan layer). The convention and separator should be a single shared helper/constant rather than re-implemented inline here.

Job Priority is the magic number 25, but a JobPriorities constants class already exists (Application/Jobs/JobPriorities.cs: Scan=60...AutoIdentify=20, Preview=10) and is used by every other enqueue site. 25 sits between AutoIdentify(20) and Sidecar(30) with no named meaning; this should reference a JobPriorities member (or add one) so priorities stay coherent and discoverable.

ItemQueryFrom hand-parses ~13 Jellyfin query keys from HttpRequest.Query inside the API endpoint layer (with the TryGuid/TryInt/TryBool/SplitCsv helpers also living here). This request->JellyfinItemQuery translation is application-protocol logic that arguably belongs beside JellyfinCatalogService rather than in the route-mapping file, and several keys (Limit/StartIndex) are re-read independently in .Catalog.cs handlers, so the parsing is split between two files.

Problem codes entity_not_found, invalid_entity, entity_not_creatable, entity_not_deletable (lines ~120,121,137,138,156,168) are free-form string literals on ApiProblem (record ApiProblem(string Code, string Message)). entity_not_found in particular is duplicated across many endpoint files (Identify*, EntityImageAssetEndpoint, EntityDetailEndpoint, EntityEndpointResults). With no shared ProblemCodes constant set, the wire error vocabulary can drift silently; consider a typed/const problem-code catalog. (Movie/MusicArtist consume these via the shared helper.)

The list endpoint exposes free-form `string? sort`, `string? sortDir`, and `string? status` query parameters that are forwarded untyped into IEntityReadService.ListAsync. These are sort/filter/status dispatch keys (closed sets) handled as raw strings at the edge with no decode/validation here, the same pattern the codec system exists to eliminate for job-type/entity-kind. Worth confirming they are validated/typed downstream; if not, they are candidate magic-string keys.

Internal route-prefix vocabulary (/api, /api/health, /assets, /openapi) is duplicated as bare literals across three files: auth gating (PrismediaAuthentication), static-file/codegen mapping (Program.cs), and the dev proxy (SpaDevProxy.ApiPrefixes). Jellyfin routes were already centralized into JellyfinRoutes/JellyfinProtocol; the Prismedia-side route prefixes deserve the same single source of truth to keep auth, proxy, and static-serving in agreement.

ApiProblem error codes are scattered as inline string literals across every endpoint module (plugin_not_found, setting_not_found, jellyfin_profile_not_found, video_subtitle_not_found, missing_api_key, etc.) with several repeated within a single file. There is no central problem-code catalog, so codes can't be referenced by tests/clients and risk typos. A shared ApiProblemCodes constants class (ideally surfaced to the frontend via codegen) would match the project's canonical-identifier direction.

Image-role priority/ordering logic is duplicated between ProjectImages (lines 199-219) and ToThumbnail (lines 234-247): the same role filter set (Thumbnail/Poster/Cover/Backdrop/Logo), the same ImageSourcePriority OrderBy, and the same role-rank ThenBy switch are copy-pasted. A single shared 'best image' / 'ordered cover assets' helper would prevent the two from drifting.

HoverKind ('none'/'sprite') and EntityThumbnailMeta.Icon vocabulary ('video'/'image') are wire scalars hardcoded here AND independently in Infrastructure's EfEntityReadService.Thumbnails.cs (lines 112, 235-240). Two projectors are supposed to produce identical thumbnail shapes (this file's own doc-comment calls the row path 'the one deliberate read-optimized exception'), but they share no constant for these codes, so they can silently diverge.

ListAsync has 23 parameters (a cancellationToken wedged mid-list at position 7 followed by 14 optional filters). This is a parameter-object smell: the filter set (sort/dir/seed/favorite/organized/rating/status/bookType/bookFormat/nsfw/hasFile/played/orphaned) should be a single EntityListQuery options record, both for readability and so new filters don't keep extending the signature.

Fan-out N+1 / unbounded fetch risk: RecursiveMusicItemsAsync eagerly loads ALL artists and ALL albums into in-memory dictionaries (FetchAllOfKindAsync paging to MaxBrowseItems=5000) on every recursive music query, and ChildrenOfAsync recursive-series path issues a GetAsync per season. Combined with per-id GetItemAsync loops (ItemsByIdAsync) this can be many sequential DB round-trips per browse request; noted as a known perf area in comments but worth flagging.

Cross-layer sort/status vocabulary drift: the bare literals 'added', 'title', 'last-played', 'in-progress', 'asc'/'desc' are produced here and consumed by EfEntityReadService (Infrastructure) with no shared constant. The Jellyfin layer is effectively the keyboard for the entity read service's string contract; these should be typed/constant on the IEntityReadService boundary.

The composite 'archive::member' source-path format is an implicit cross-layer contract: this handler splits on '::' and the scan/exclusion code (LibraryScanPathRules.cs) builds/matches on '::', but there is no shared parser/value-type owning the convention. The handler also assumes the archive is specifically a Zip (ZipFile.OpenRead), so CBR/RAR-style comic archives would silently fail extraction despite the generic 'book page' framing.

Subtitle metadata is recovered by re-parsing the extracted filename (split on '-', positional language at [1], index at [2]) instead of carrying the stream info through from ExtractSubtitlesAsync. This couples this handler to the Infrastructure file-naming scheme; a naming change in the asset generator breaks language/index assignment with no compile-time link. Prefer returning structured (path, language, index, codec) tuples from the generator.

Four near-identical private path helpers are copy-pasted across the scan handlers: SamePath, NormalizePath, and SiblingSortOrders are duplicated verbatim in ScanAudio and ScanGallery; PathDepth/IsBelowRoot logic mirrors AudioLibraryClassifier's Depth/IsStrictDescendant; ScanLibrary has its own SamePath/IsPathUnderRoot. This path-normalization logic should live in one shared helper (e.g. a ScanPathUtil in Jobs/Scanning) so the handlers compose instead of each re-deriving root-relative path math.

The 'honor per-root AutoIdentify opt-out' block (settings = settings with { AutoIdentifyEnabled = false }) is copy-pasted identically into all four scan handlers. Pull this into ScanJobHandler base (e.g. an effective-settings accessor) so the opt-out is applied in one place and cannot drift between media types.

This per-kind downstream-job fan-out (video->probe/fingerprint/subtitles/preview; image->fingerprint/thumbnail; audio-track->probe/fingerprint/waveform; book-page->thumbnail) duplicates the chaining logic that already lives in each Scan*JobHandler. The 'what downstream jobs does kind X need' decision now has two owners (refresh vs scan) that must be kept in sync by hand; consider a shared downstream-job planner keyed by EntityKind that both call.

Large multi-responsibility handler (~485 lines): archive discovery, single-file book discovery, hierarchy building, ComicInfo metadata selection, stale reconciliation, plus an embedded NaturalPathComparer and two nested record DTOs. The NaturalPathComparer in particular is a general-purpose utility embedded as a private nested class; it duplicates natural-sort intent that other scanners (which sort by OrdinalIgnoreCase) lack and should be a shared comparer.

CollectionRuleCondition carries EntityTypes/Field/Operator as raw strings to mirror the TypeScript contract. These are dispatch/key identifiers (entity-kind codes, field keys, operator codes) interpreted by the infrastructure engine. Within these files there is no validation or canonical-code reference, so invalid/unknown field/operator/entity-type values are only caught (or silently dropped) deep in the engine. The actual literal-vs-code coupling lives in Infrastructure/Collections/CollectionRuleEngine.cs (out of this cluster) and should be audited there for hardcoded entity-kind/field/operator strings.

MediaStreamProbeData.Type is a bare 'string' for a closed set of stream kinds (audio/video/subtitle). Consumers in Infrastructure compare it against capitalized literals (HlsAssetService/VideoSourceService/ThumbnailService do stream.Type.Equals("Audio"/"Video", OrdinalIgnoreCase)), so the closed-set value has no canonical [Code] enum backing and the casing contract is implicit. Consider a coded MediaStreamKind enum (EnumCodec) so the type is typed end-to-end and the comparison literals disappear.

IdentifyApplyProgress.State is modeled as a free-form string at every layer (this store + the Contracts record IdentifyQueueContracts.cs:57). The terminal-state logic does a string compare (state == "succeeded" at line 155) and the same three literals are repeated in 7 places. A closed lifecycle (running/succeeded/failed) with no enum/const is fragile; a typo in any one site silently breaks progress reporting.

The registry duplicates closed-set vocabularies that already (PlaybackMode) or should (visibility mode, subtitle style, transcoder profile, quality preset) exist as canonical [Code] enums, re-typing them as raw SettingOption id strings. Per the project's canonical-identifier system these option ids should be sourced from the enum codes so the settings select and the runtime consumers cannot diverge; today a renamed enum code would not update the setting option and the value would fail to resolve at the consumer.

LanguageAliases is a private hardcoded ISO-639 alias table duplicated logic-wise with audio language matching. The same language-normalization concern almost certainly exists in subtitle/audio selection elsewhere (settings expose PreferredLanguages for both audio and subtitles); this alias map and NormalizeLanguageToken/SelectPreferredAudioStream should live in one shared language-matching helper rather than being embedded in the playback negotiator.

Application layer hand-builds API URL paths (/Videos/{id}/master.m3u8, /Videos/{id}/hls/remux/stream.m3u8) and query string keys. This is a layering leak: the Application use-case service encodes the API host's route table and Jellyfin query-key vocabulary as string templates, so a route rename in Prismedia.Api silently breaks playback with no compile-time link. Route/query construction should be a shared contract (or injected URL builder).

Transcode/remux codec+container negotiation hardcodes the output ladder (remux=mp4/hevc/aac copy-or-stereo, transcode=ts/h264/aac) as bare literals split across PlaybackInfoService and the actual ffmpeg pipeline in Infrastructure (HlsAssetService.Remux/Encoding). The advertised TranscodingInfoResult and the real ffmpeg output can drift independently because they share no constants; e.g. advertised video codec falls back to "hevc" while the negotiator may pick a different real codec.

BuildStreams projects VideoSourceStream -> MediaStreamInfoResult with ~22 positional args and is near-identical mapping logic to JellyfinCatalogService.MediaStreams.cs (which already uses JellyfinProtocol.MediaTypes constants). Two parallel stream-to-DTO mappers risk divergence; consider a single shared stream projection.

Container/codec normalization tables (NormalizeContainerToken: matroska/mkv->mkv, mp4/m4v/mov->mp4, ts/mpegts/m2ts->ts; NormalizeCodecToken: hevc/h264/eac3/ac3 aliases) are private to this policy. These same container-family and codec-alias mappings are needed by HLS planning, the transcode pipeline, and ProbeVideoJobHandler; keeping them private here is a duplication/drift risk. Consider a shared MediaContainer/MediaCodec normalization helper (media-core or a domain value type) so wire->canonical mapping has one home.

ffprobe color-transfer/primaries wire codes (arib-std-b67, smpte2084, bt2020) are duplicated verbatim between this Application classifier and Prismedia.Infrastructure/Media/Processing/FfmpegToneMapping.cs (lines 63-66). The two must stay in lockstep (classification feeds tone-map selection) but share no constant, so a change in one layer silently diverges from the other. The doc comment already claims this overload is 'the single source of truth for HDR detection' — extracting the wire codes into a shared constant set would make that true at the literal level too.

EntityThumbnail is a wide flat record (16 positional ctor params plus 7 init-only properties). The positional/init split makes call sites order-sensitive and the record hard to evolve; many fields (Kind, HoverKind, ParentKind, Group.Code, Meta.Icon) are stringly-typed codes that duplicate domain enums. Consider grouping the stringly-typed code fields under typed wrappers or at least documenting which canonical enum each maps to.

OrganizePlanItem.Status is a bare string whose closed set (ready/unchanged/skipped/applied/failed) is documented only in the XML comment. The producer (OrganizeService.cs) defines these as private consts (Ready/Unchanged/... at lines 13-17), so the contract has no shared/typed home and the Svelte consumer must hardcode the same five literals — drift risk. Should be an [Code] enum (e.g. OrganizePlanStatus) projected to a typed code, mirroring the EntityKind/JobType pattern.

ApiProblem.Code is the project-wide machine-readable error code envelope yet is an open string with no shared problem-code registry/enum. Error codes are inherently a closed dispatch set used by clients for branching; without a canonical [Code] enum (or generated codes.ts const), every producer invents codes and every consumer hardcodes them. Recommend a ProblemCode enum + codec so codes are generated into the frontend alongside the other code families.

UpdateCheckResponse.Status (available/current/unknown/development) and Channel (dev/alpha/beta/release) are documented closed sets typed as string. Channel in particular is a real dispatch/identifier value that also appears in the release/publish tooling; both should reference shared enums/[Code] so server, update-checker, and frontend stay aligned rather than reproducing the spelling.

MediaSourceInfo.Protocol, TranscodingSubProtocol, MediaStreamInfo.Type, VideoRange, VideoRangeType, TranscodingInfo.Protocol are Jellyfin wire scalars typed as string. JellyfinProtocol (Contracts/Jellyfin) is the declared single source of truth for Jellyfin wire vocab but does NOT yet define stream-type ('Video'/'Audio'/'Subtitle'), protocol ('File'/'Http'), transcoding subprotocol ('hls'), or video-range ('SDR'/'HDR10'/'DOVI') constants. The producer (PlaybackInfoService.cs lines 91/108/115 and VideoPlaybackRangePolicy) emits these as raw literals. These vocabularies should be added to JellyfinProtocol so the producer references them instead of retyping; the contract fields then carry a documented protocol-constant vocabulary rather than a free string.

IdentifyQueueItem.EntityKind, State (search/proposal/done/deleted/error), Action, and Provider are bare strings; State is an explicitly closed set documented only in the XML comment, and is duplicated again by IdentifyApplyProgress.State (running/succeeded/failed, line 56). These are queue-state dispatch codes with no shared enum/[Code], so both the queue service and the review UI hardcode them — a clear drift risk for a closed status set.

EntityMetadataPatch uses several string-keyed dictionaries for closed code families: Dates, Stats, Positions, plus CreditPatch.Role and ImageCandidate.Kind, and EntityMetadataPatch.Classification. Role corresponds to the CreditRole [Code] enum and image Kind to an image-asset-kind code, but the patch shape carries them as raw strings / dictionary keys, so plugins and the apply pipeline must agree on key spelling by convention. Worth documenting which keys map to which canonical code family (CreditRole, position codes, stat keys) or using typed wrappers.

PluginEntitySupport.Actions / PluginManifest action vocab (lookup-id, lookup-url, search, cascade), Runtime ('dotnet-process'), and EntityKind are closed code sets documented in comments but typed as string. IdentifyPluginRequest.Action and IdentifyEntityRequest.Provider (IdentifyRequests.cs) consume the same action/provider vocab. There is no shared plugin-action enum, so the runtime, manifest loader, and each plugin reproduce these literals — drift risk across the plugin protocol boundary.

VideoDetail.CreditMetadata uses EntityCreditMetadata (defined in Entities/EntityCards.cs:7) whose Role property is a raw `string?`. A coded CreditRole enum exists at Prismedia.Domain/Entities/Enums/Taxonomy/CreditRole.cs with the EnumCodec/[Code] treatment, yet the contract carries Role as an untyped string, so the canonical code is stringly-typed at the API boundary (drift risk between domain enum and wire value). The root cause is in EntityCards.cs (outside this cluster), but VideoDetail is the consumer that surfaces it. Medium severity.

CapabilitySource.Item.Code is a bare `string` whose XML doc enumerates a closed 'stable source code' vocabulary (library-root, folder, relative, file, archive, zip) but there is no [Code] enum or shared const backing it. Unlike the sibling FileSourceKind enum (which uses [Code("scan")]/[Code("custom")] as the single source of truth), this vocabulary is documentation-only, so the actual literals live untyped in consumers (e.g. Infrastructure/Entities/Mappers/Capabilities/SourceCapabilityMapper.cs, scan persistence) with no compile-time guard against drift. Should be a [Code] enum like the rest of the canonical identifier system.

CapabilityStats.Item.Code is a bare `string` documented as a closed 'stable statistic code' set (images, tracks, pages, chapters, items) with no backing enum/[Code]. Same untyped-vocabulary drift risk as CapabilitySource: callers must hand-write the literal and the upsert match is a plain Ordinal string compare. Should reference a typed code set.

EntityDate.Code and EntityDate.Precision are bare `string`s documented as closed vocabularies (Code: release/air/birth/career-start; Precision: year/month/day/text) but neither has a [Code] enum or shared const. Used as upsert/match keys in CapabilityDates.Set (Ordinal compare) and shared across CapabilityLifetime, so the same untyped date-code/precision-code strings get hardcoded at every producer. Should become typed codes consistent with the EnumCodec/[Code] system.

Progress 'unit' is a free-form string ('item' default here, 'page' passed from Book.cs, re-normalized to 'item' in EntityCapabilityService.cs:175). Note 'mode' here is correctly fed via ReaderMode.ToCode() at Book.cs:48 (codec used), so the file mixes a properly-coded value (mode) with an uncoded sibling (unit) on the same MoveTo call — making the gap easy to miss and inconsistent within one method.

Provider is a free-form string provider code in the domain record, but the frontend has a generated EXTERNAL_ID_PROVIDER set and the codebase has a provider/source id vocabulary. There is no domain-side [Code] enum or const enforcing the provider vocabulary at the SetExternalId boundary (Entity.cs:126), so provider spelling is unguarded where identities are written.

MoveReaderToChapter passes the bare unit literal "page" into CapabilityProgress.MoveTo, while EntityMetadataPositionRules.cs separately hardcodes the same 'page'/'track'/'chapter'/'volume' position vocabulary. CapabilityProgress.Unit is an untyped free-form string, so the progress-unit vocabulary is unconstrained and drifts across the codebase with no single owner. Correctness/drift risk if the two sites diverge.

TranslateStudioRelation duplicates ~90% of TranslateRelation (subquery shape, in/not_in handling, names guard) plus its own is_null/is_not_null EXISTS branches; studio uses the entity kind "studio" while tags/performers pass a taxonomyKindCode. The two should be unified into one relation translator parameterized by relationship+target-kind to eliminate the copy.

ListAsync has 22 parameters (kind/query/cursor/hideNsfw/limit + 17 optional filters) and ApplyListFilters takes 13; this is a parameter-explosion smell. The filter set should be a single ListFilters request record so new filters do not keep widening the signature and so the same shape can be shared with the contract layer.

Cover-source priority is decided partly by path-substring matching ("/custom/artwork/", "/plugins/artwork/") rather than purely by the FileSourceKind code on the row. This couples cover ranking to physical path layout produced by EntityImageAssetMutationService, so a storage-path change in one file silently changes ranking in the other; the two literals are duplicated across the files with no shared constant.

FormatResolution (lines 276-283) reimplements the resolution-tier vocabulary (4K/1440p/1080p/720p/480p) that CollectionRuleEngine.ResolutionMap also defines (with different boundary values: thumbnails use >=2160 for 4K while the rule engine uses 2160..99999 and adds no 1440p tier). Two independent, slightly inconsistent definitions of the same concept.

Hydration performs synchronous filesystem I/O (Path.IsPathRooted + File.Exists at lines 16-17) inside an async DB hydration path. This couples persistence hydration to disk state, makes hydration non-deterministic (rows silently disappear if the file is missing/unmounted), and blocks the thread per-row. Consider validating file presence in a dedicated read/projection step rather than during capability hydration.

ProjectDetail (lines 73-131) is a growing EntityKind switch that hardcodes one branch per kind, each copying the same 7-8 EntityCard fields into a *Detail DTO. Every new detail-bearing kind must edit this switch and duplicate the property-copy block; the copy boilerplate is itself error-prone (a missed field silently drops data). A shared 'copy card -> detail' helper plus a registry/descriptor-driven detail factory would remove both the switch growth and the field-copy duplication. Also VideoSeason is special-cased twice (ConstructAsync line 55 and ProjectDetail line 119), splitting its handling.

The ChipIcon switch maps EntityKind codes to thumbnail icon glyphs as bare string literals, and the SAME icon vocabulary is independently hardcoded in EfEntityReadService.Thumbnails.cs (lines ~227-242: 'book','video','image','audio') and typed as a closed union EntityThumbnailMetaIcon on the frontend (apps/web-svelte/src/lib/entities/entity-thumbnail.ts:45) consumed by EntityThumbnail.svelte. There is no single backend source of truth for this glyph set, so backend producers and the frontend union can drift (a kind mapping to an icon the frontend union/switch doesn't render would silently fall through). Recommend a shared ThumbnailIcons constant set on the backend, and ideally include the icon glyph in EntityKindMeta so ChipIcon can be derived from EntityKindRegistry.Describe(kind) instead of a parallel hand-maintained switch.

ApplyPathPrefixRewriteAsync loads ALL EntityFiles with Role==Source into memory (no path filter) and iterates them to remap moved paths. On large libraries this materializes the entire source-file catalog per move; could be bounded by a Like(scopePrefix + '%') predicate like the other queries in this file do.

ExpandContainerSubtreeIdsAsync loads the entire Entities table (Id + ParentEntityId) into memory to build a parent->children map for every stale-container removal. This is an unbounded full-table scan that scales with total library size, not with the removed subtree; consider a recursive CTE or scoping to the affected root.

Pervasive per-kind upsert duplication: ~10 near-identical UpsertXAsync methods repeat the same find-by-source-path / update-tracked-entity / else create EntityRow+detail+source-file block (video, image, gallery, audio track/library, music artist, book/series/volume/chapter/page). The find-or-create-entity-with-source-file skeleton and the structural-child-link tail could be factored into a shared helper to cut the copy-paste and reduce the chance one kind diverges (e.g. SizeBytes is populated for files but null for folder-backed kinds, applied inconsistently).

DeleteGeneratedPreviewFiles hardcodes a parallel set of cache directory segments ("videos","thumb.jpg","preview.mp4","sprite.jpg","trickplay.vtt","trickplay-frames","trickplay","hlsv","hls2","hls","images","book-pages","audio-tracks","waveform.json") that duplicate the path construction already centralized in AssetPathService. These are CSS-free file path segments (not flagged as magic identifiers), but the duplication means a path change in AssetPathService would silently leave stale files behind; deletion should route through AssetPathService. Also the "hlsv"/"hls2"/"hls" legacy variants are only known here.

ApplyPathPrefixRewriteAsync loads ALL EntityFiles where Role==Source and ALL EntitySources into memory (full table materialization), then filters/mutates client-side per row. On a large library this pulls the entire source-file table into memory for every organize move. Consider a targeted query by path prefix (StartsWith) or a set-based ExecuteUpdate where feasible, since the rewrite only touches rows under sourcePath.

Cross-file duplication of the probe stream-type dispatch literal 'Video'/'Audio': the same .Type.Equals("Video"/"Audio") filtering is re-implemented in ThumbnailService, HlsAssetService.Encoding, HlsAssetService.Remux, HlsAssetService, and VideoSourceService with no shared constant. The Type value is minted in MediaProbeService.cs:60. One stringly-typed contract spread across 5+ infra files invites drift; promote to a shared constant (JellyfinProtocol.MediaStreamType already has Video/Audio) or make MediaStreamProbeResult.Type a typed enum.

NeedsToneMapping in ThumbnailService duplicates the exact SDR-detection logic and 'SDR' literal already living in VideoPlaybackRangePolicy (and re-duplicated in HlsAssetService.Encoding.cs:347). The classification + 'is not SDR' check should be a single shared helper (e.g. VideoPlaybackRange.IsHdr) so tonemap decisions cannot diverge between thumbnail generation and HLS encoding.

Grab-bag file holding ~12 unrelated EF row POCOs (ignore list, UI prefs, app settings, security, Jellyfin profile/session, provider config/credential, identify result/queue, fingerprint). Several persist closed-set identifiers as plain strings with literal defaults (Action='search', Kind='file', Reason='deleted-from-library', plus Algorithm/ProviderCode/MatchType/CredentialKey free-form strings) where typed [Code] enums exist or should exist. Splitting by bounded slice and routing the code-valued columns through EnumCodec would align with the canonical identifier system.

The relationship-kind set {person, studio, tag} is asserted in at least three places: EntityMetadataProposalTraversal.IsRelationshipKind (canonical helper), a duplicated inline pattern-match in ApplyRelationshipProposalsAsync (Relationships.cs:289), and again via the helper elsewhere. The inline copy in Relationships.cs should call IsRelationshipKind so the set is defined once.

The Upsert* vs Replace* method pairs (description, externalIds, urls, dates, stats, positions, classification) plus the field-key dispatch are largely parallel across the scoped/selected/cascade apply paths (ApplyScopedPatchToEntityAsync, ApplyAsync proposal path, ApplyPatchToEntityAsync). Three separate hand-maintained dispatch ladders over the same EntityMetadataPatch fields invite divergence (e.g. 'rating' and 'flags' are handled in some paths but not others). Worth unifying behind one field-driven applier.

EntityMetadataProposal 'shell' for the candidates branch is constructed with null! for ProposalId, Provider, TargetKind, and Patch (non-nullable record members forced null). Downstream code (e.g. EnsureMeaningfulTitle, MarkMatureClassificationNsfw) null-checks Patch, but any path that dereferences ProposalId/TargetKind/Provider on a candidate shell would NRE. The null-forgiving construction hides a contract gap.

Compatibility/version logic is duplicated: IsCompatible(manifest) + ParseVersion here mirror PluginCompatibilityResolver.IsCompatible(entry)/SupportsAppVersion + its own ParseVersion. Two ParseVersion implementations and two compat gates (one for local manifests, one for index entries) can diverge in runtime/tag/version handling.

UpdateAsync calls PullProviderAsync then unconditionally re-runs full InstallAsync, which itself re-discovers and may re-pull; install/update responsibilities overlap and do redundant discovery/index fetches. Also SaveAuthAsync calls InstallAsync purely to ensure a config row, conflating credential save with provider install.

Hand-rolled indentation/section YAML parser with fixed indent levels (0/2/4/6/8) and manual scalar dispatch. Fragile against tabs, multi-line scalars, or differing indentation; the repo already uses YamlDotNet for stash scrapers, so this bespoke parser is both duplicative and a correctness risk.

When no HttpClient is injected it news up a bare new HttpClient() per instance (same in PluginCatalogService.cs:58). For a scoped/transient infrastructure service this risks socket exhaustion; these should flow through IHttpClientFactory / a shared typed client.

DownloadPluginImageAsync derives the stored role code via role.ToString().ToLowerInvariant() instead of role.ToCode(); EnumCodec codes are not guaranteed to equal the lowercased enum name (e.g. grid-thumbnail), so the on-disk filename code can diverge from the canonical EntityFileRole code used elsewhere (DownloadSelectedImagesAsync passes the real roleCode).

The 'ByURL' capability-suffix convention is reimplemented via EndsWith in multiple places (ResolveFetchUrl, ScrapeProposalAsync) and capability vocabulary is split across StashScraperDefinition.CapabilityKeys, StashScraperManifestFactory.CapabilityMap, and inline literals in runner/engine. The Stash capability vocabulary should live in one place with helpers (IsByUrl, etc.) to prevent divergence.

Stash JSON field-name vocabulary (title/code/details/url/image/performers/tags/studio/name/etc.) is parsed independently in StashScriptExecutor (python stdout) and StashJsonEngine (jsonScrapers), with subtly different handling (urls/images array fallbacks only in the executor). Duplicated decode logic over the same external schema risks drift between the two scrape paths.

The HEVC/AV1/VP9/H265 codec-classification predicate is reimplemented three times with bare string literals: IsEfficientVideoCodec (Encoding.cs:323), IsHevcCodec (Remux.cs:672), IsHdrProneCodec (VideoSourceService.cs:244). No shared codec constant or predicate exists. Drift risk: each list can fall out of sync (e.g. Encoding includes av1/vp9, Remux's HEVC check does not, by design — but the overlap is undocumented and copy-pasted). Extract a shared video-codec constant set + predicate helpers.

Cache root names ("hlsv","hls","hls2") are defined independently in two places: TranscodeCacheService.CacheRootNames lists all three; HlsAssetService.CandidatePackageRoots (line 113) only enumerates hls2+hls for legacy lookup and VirtualRoot hardcodes "hlsv" separately (line 537). These must stay aligned for eviction to actually cover what the asset service writes; a single shared constant would prevent a root being evicted-but-not-found or written-but-not-evicted.

The 26-field VideoSourceStream projection is written out twice verbatim — once mapping MediaStreams rows (line 85) and once mapping freshly-probed streams (line 117). Any new stream field must be added in both lists or the re-probe path silently drops it. A single mapping helper (from a common stream shape) would remove the duplication.

HlsAssetService is a single sealed partial class spanning 4 files (~1600 lines) that mixes HTTP-asset routing, ffmpeg argument construction, process orchestration, on-disk cache lifecycle, static cross-request job registries (ActiveRenditions/RemuxGenerations as static ConcurrentDictionaries), and reaper coordination. The static shared-state design couples it to TranscodeCacheService and TranscodeSessionService via static method calls (CancelActiveGenerationsForItem, ReapOrphanedJobs) rather than an injected job-registry abstraction, making the lifecycle hard to test and the partial-class boundary the only seam. Consider extracting an injectable transcode-job registry.

Test inlines the exact cache-control string the hook sets via PAGE_CACHE_CONTROL (lines 22 and 38) instead of importing the constant. If the policy value changes in cache-policy.ts, the hook updates automatically but the test keeps asserting the old string, so the test would fail for the wrong reason (or, worse, mask intent). Import PAGE_CACHE_CONTROL into the test and assert against it.

buildHrefWithFrom is duplicated verbatim as a private function in src/lib/video-card-data.ts:47-51 (identical `from=${encodeURIComponent(from)}` logic). video-card-data.ts should import the exported helper here instead of re-implementing it, so the back-navigation `from` contract stays in one place.

Two parallel mappers (entityCardToVideoCardData and videoListItemToCardData) produce the same VideoCardData but diverge: the EntityThumbnail path hardcodes rating*20, tags:[], codec:undefined and uses meta-icon scraping, while the VideoLike path uses typed fields directly. Risk of inconsistent card rendering depending on which list feeds the grid; the EntityThumbnail branch in particular is lossy (drops codec/tags/performers).

Card-vs-poster thumbnail selection keys off a magic path substring ("thumb-custom"). This couples the view-model to a backend file-naming convention; if the backend changes the custom thumbnail filename, custom thumbnails silently regress to showing the auto card thumbnail.

Correctness/drift: IdentifyQueueItem here declares cascadeRunning as a required boolean (line 144), but the identify-client.test.ts queueItem fixture (and the wire payload it mimics) omits cascadeRunning entirely. Because results are cast 'as IdentifyQueueItem' rather than validated, a missing cascadeRunning silently becomes undefined at runtime despite the type claiming it is always present.

Dead/stub helpers: getTags() always returns [] regardless of capabilities (line 59-61), so any caller relying on it for tag data gets nothing. Likely a leftover stub after the capability refactor; should be removed or implemented.

Layering inconsistency: this module mixes two transports. Most mutations go through the generated client (updateEntityRatingRequest etc.), but uploadEntityImageAsset/clearEntityImageAsset bypass it and hit raw paths via uploadFile/fetchApi ('/entities/${id}/images/${role}'). The image endpoints exist in the generated client elsewhere; using raw string paths here loses the generated URL/codegen guarantee and hardcodes the route shape.

Type duplication: EntityMetadataPatch/EntityMetadataUpdateRequest are declared here AND a near-identical EntityMetadataPatch is declared in identify-types.ts (different credits typing: unknown[] here vs CreditPatch[] there). Two competing patch shapes for the same backend contract invites divergence; both should derive from one generated/shared type.

Jellyfin DTO interfaces (JellyfinPlaybackInfoRequest, JellyfinMediaStreamInfo, JellyfinTranscodingInfo, JellyfinMediaSourceInfo, JellyfinPlaybackInfoResponse, JellyfinPlaybackSessionRequest, JellyfinDeviceProfile-adjacent) are declared in full in BOTH prismedia.ts and playback.ts, and they already drift: playback.ts adds JellyfinDeviceProfile/DirectPlayProfiles + SupportedVideoRangeTypes/DeviceProfile and IsDefault/IsForced are non-null booleans, while prismedia.ts's copy lacks DeviceProfile and makes IsDefault/IsForced nullable. These should be one shared module (ideally generated from the backend Jellyfin contracts).

Layering/consistency: prismedia.ts reaches for raw fetchApi on several endpoints that have generated clients used elsewhere (e.g. fetchWorkerHealth -> fetchApi("/health/worker"), updateEntityProgress -> fetchApi("/entities/{id}/progress")), bypassing the generated-client + unwrapGenerated convention the rest of the file follows. plugins.ts similarly hand-rolls ~30 fetchApi routes (/scrapers/*, /stashbox-endpoints/*, /stash-ids, /plugins/packages/*) with no generated client, so those URLs/methods are unchecked against the backend contract.

LibrarySettings property names (visibilityDefaultMode, autoScanEnabled, scanIntervalMinutes, subtitleStyle, hlsTranscoderProfile, etc.) mirror the camelCase keys of the canonical SETTING_KEYS const in generated/codes.ts but the interface does not derive from or reference it, so adding/renaming a setting key requires manually keeping this interface, SETTING_KEYS, app-settings.ts defaults, and the catalog mappers in sync — drift risk across 3+ files.

Mixed transport: most calls go through the generated orval client (getSettings, updateSetting, etc.) but fetchSettingsValues, fetchTranscodeCacheStatus, and clearTranscodeCache bypass it and hand-build raw fetchApi URL strings ("/settings/values", "/settings/transcode-cache", "/settings/transcode-cache/clear"). These endpoints are untyped against the OpenAPI contract and the path strings are bare literals with no shared route constant.

The dynamic-rule model (CollectionRuleNode tree, CollectionOperator, COLLECTION_RULE_FIELDS, field/operator vocab) is defined entirely on the frontend with no visible shared contract to the backend rule engine. ruleTreeJson is serialized as an opaque string (line 108), so field names ('title','rating','videoSeriesId',...), operators, and enum values must match a backend evaluator by convention only — a fragile cross-boundary string contract with no generated/typed source of truth.

Duplicated pointer-gesture logic: ReaderPointerGesture interface, READER_SWIPE_THRESHOLD=50, the 10px move-slop, swipe vs tap resolution, and the comicTapZone dispatch are reimplemented almost identically in both readers. This is parallel copy-paste of non-trivial interaction logic; extract a shared reader-gesture helper/action so behavior can't drift between book and comic readers.

Duplicated CSS: .reader-mode-button, .reader-nav-button, .active-reader-control, hover/focus glow states, and the >=640px arrow-visibility media query are copied between the two reader components instead of living in a shared stylesheet or the ReaderShell component. Style drift risk; consolidate into shared reader styles.

API access bypasses the generated orval client: the player builds raw route strings (/audio-tracks/<id>/play, /audio-stream/<id>) and calls fetch directly via apiAssetUrl, rather than using the typed generated audio client. CLAUDE.md says the frontend should prefer the generated OpenAPI client; these inline paths can silently 404 on a backend route rename (the file is named *VidStack* but uses a plain <audio> element, also slightly misleading).

studio and credits are permanently stubbed (`(): ... => null` / `=> []`), so the heroMeta studio link and the entire afterBody People/credits section are dead code that can never render. Either wire them to real image capabilities/credits or remove the dead branches and the unused resolveEntityHref("studio"/"person") calls.

Modal uses static-div click handlers with multiple a11y suppressions and no focus trap; Escape is handled via a window keydown listener but there is no role=dialog/aria-modal (unlike the sibling modals), so it is inconsistent and less accessible than ConfirmDeleteDialog/DestinationPicker. Consolidating all four modals onto one Dialog primitive would fix this.

Two independent searchable-popover-select implementations exist. ProviderSelector hand-rolls document-level mousedown click-outside (instead of the shared keepFlyoutOnScreen action SearchSelect uses) and duplicates the trigger/search/option-row markup. Consolidating onto one primitive removes ~140 lines of duplicated select logic and unifies keyboard/click-outside behavior.

Single 1089-line component mixing pdf.js loading, lazy IntersectionObserver rendering, text/link layer DOM construction, pinch/tap gesture handling, in-document search/text-indexing, and TOC. Multiple 'any'-typed pdf.js handles and inline ESLint disables.
