The rendering pipeline, gesture handling, and search index would be better factored into separate modules/actions for testability; correctness-sensitive (resume relies on placeholder offsets) and hard to unit test as one blob.

Lightbox owns a parallel rating implementation (ratingOverrides map, handleRate clear-on-reclick, inline star Buttons) that duplicates StarRatingPicker's logic and the same 0-5 semantics. Two rating codepaths can drift (e.g. clear-on-reclick, aria). Prefer composing StarRatingPicker.

handlePointerUp contains two large, near-duplicated swipe-gesture branches (video vs image): both compute dx/dy/elapsed and the same 'elapsed<700 && max>60 then absX>absY*1.3' swipe-to-navigate / 'dy>0' swipe-to-dismiss thresholds. The magic gesture thresholds (700, 60, 1.3, 6, DOUBLE_TAP_MS) are repeated; extract a shared classifySwipe() to avoid divergence.

Local formatTime and languageLabel reimplement helpers that already exist in ./video-player-format.ts (used by VideoPlayer and VideoSettingsMenu). Two divergent time/language formatters risk inconsistent output (e.g. 'und' handling, hour formatting). Consolidate on the shared video-player-format helpers.

~2089-line component is a god-component: source/HLS-readiness polling, direct/HLS/forced-transcode fallback state machine, audio/quality/subtitle selection, cast framework loading, fullscreen, media-session, keyboard shortcuts, and native+Vidstack event bridging all in one file. Several concerns (cast loading, playback-mode state machine, the dual native/Vidstack event sync) are independently testable and could move to $lib/player modules to shrink the component.

Playback state is tracked redundantly across the MediaPlayerElement, the underlying native <video>, and component $state (playing/muted/volume/buffering), with parallel Vidstack listeners (lines 1297-1322) and native listeners (lines 1324-1404) maintaining the same fields. This dual-source bookkeeping is fragile and a frequent source of desync; worth a single normalization layer.

Many tests assert on component internals by reading the .svelte source text with readFile and string-matching CSS/markup (e.g. expecting '.prismedia-player-surface:fullscreen', 'aspect-ratio: 16 / 9'). These source-string assertions are brittle white-box tests that break on harmless refactors/formatting; prefer behavioral/DOM assertions.

Cue-list rendering (current/past/future styling, data-cue-index, seek-on-click) is duplicated between the compact variant and the full variant with only minor class differences, so behavior changes must be made twice. Extract one shared cue-row/list.

CollectionOperator, the rule-group operator, and CollectionRuleFieldDef.fieldType exist only as bare TS type unions with no value-level constant. Consumers (ConditionBuilder, CollectionEditor) therefore hardcode each operator/fieldType string in dispatch logic with no shared constant to reference, so the unions and the runtime checks can drift apart.

defaultValue (ConditionBuilder) and isConditionRunnable (CollectionEditor) encode overlapping operator/fieldType semantics in two places (e.g. both special-case between/in/not_in and number/date/enum). The validity rules for a condition value live in two components and can disagree; consolidate into a single rule-eval helper in $lib/collections.

The .bulk-btn style recipe is explicitly copy-pasted from EntityGridToolbar's scoped stylesheet (acknowledged in the 170-174 comment). Duplicated CSS across sibling components means toolbar restyles won't propagate here; the bulk-button family should be a shared class/component.

Component is very large (~789 lines of script) and mixes many concerns: prefs persistence, viewport measurement (two ResizeObserver onMount blocks), scroll suppression, selection, NSFW bulk mutation with optimistic capability overrides, preset CRUD, and full client-side pagination/seek-with-remote-loading. Several responsibilities (pagination/seek, viewport measurement, prefs+snapshot persistence) are independently testable units that could be extracted to keep the orchestrator thin.

Many tests assert CSS by readFileSync of EntityDetail.svelte and src/app.css and string-matching exact rules (e.g. 'height: 2.35rem;', padding-top calc strings, grid-template-area strings). These are brittle structural assertions that break on benign style edits and couple the test to source text rather than rendered behavior; prefer DOM/computed-style or visual assertions.

Flag state is read by hardcoded string codes (card.flags.find(f => f.code === "favorite"/"nsfw"/"organized")) while the canonical code union lives in entity-detail.ts with no runtime const. The producer (entity-detail.ts:258-264) and this consumer can drift; introduce a FLAG_CODE const both reference.

Very large single component (~1485 lines, ~50 props) mixing search, sort, view-mode, media-wall, thumbnail-size, filter chips, presets, and bulk-selection with scroll-driven collapse logic and a ~900-line <style> block. Hard to test/maintain; the sort-option list, view-mode set, and taxonomy-kind gating duplicate types/consts that live elsewhere. Consider splitting into sub-toolbars (SearchSort, ViewControls, BulkBar) and deriving option lists from shared constants.

renderContextMenu builds the menu with imperative DOM + inline styles + hardcoded design-token fallback strings (colors, radii, fonts), and reparents the node to document.body to escape @pierre/trees' stacking context. This duplicates the toolbar's declarative flyout, bypasses the design system, and is fragile against the third-party library's DOM contract (data-type='item', data-item-path, etc. used as selectors). Manual lifecycle (queueMicrotask append, library tears down via element.remove) is subtle and easy to break.

containerKinds/leafKinds and the resolvePreviewKind MIME branching encode domain knowledge (which entity kinds are containers vs leaves; how MIME maps to a preview kind) inline in a UI component using bare string literals. This classification logic belongs in a shared helper keyed off ENTITY_KIND so it stays consistent with the rest of the app and is unit-testable.

EntityIndexPage hardcodes entity.kind === "image" while its own state class (entity-index-page.svelte.ts) correctly uses ENTITY_KIND.video/.movie. Inconsistent: the page should reference ENTITY_KIND.image too, otherwise a kind-code rename in the canonical enum silently breaks the lightbox-image branch.

Position-code normalization/labeling (normalizePositionCodeForReview, positionCodeLabel, positionValueLabel) is a large block of string-keyed dispatch over position codes (season/episode/volume/chapter/page/track/sort) with no shared constant and partial overlap with backend position vocabulary; both the raw codes and the labels live only here. High duplication/correctness surface if backend codes change.

Flyout/listbox/keyboard-nav/add-new logic is copy-pasted across SearchSelect, TagSelect, and EntityPicker (three near-identical implementations of activeIndex math, ArrowUp/Down/Enter/Escape handling, outside-click close, and the Add-new option row). Extracting a shared combobox/listbox primitive would remove ~3x duplicated interaction code.

addNew constructs synthetic ids as `new:${title.toLowerCase()}` — a wire-significant id prefix convention ('new:') that consumers must understand to distinguish new vs existing entities. This prefix is an implicit contract with no shared constant; if the apply/save side parses it elsewhere it is a drift risk.

entityTypeLabel derives the displayed type via a regex (/^(book|comic|manga|novel)$/i) over entity.meta where item.icon === 'book'. This re-derives a book subtype on the client from a display meta item rather than reading a typed book-type field; fragile and locale/casing dependent.

The 'year' search input (searchYear) is bound and cleared but never passed to store.identifyEntity in handleSearch (only title is sent). The year field is effectively dead input — either wire it into the search request or remove it.

isActiveQueueState(state) is duplicated verbatim in two modules (both: state !== 'done' && state !== 'deleted'). Extract one shared predicate next to IDENTIFY_QUEUE_STATE so the active-state rule lives in exactly one place.

Two parallel kind->icon mappers exist: identify-icons.entityKindIcon (kind -> Lucide Component) and entity-thumbnail.iconForKind (kind -> meta-icon string). They encode the same kind groupings independently; keep both keyed on ENTITY_KIND and consider co-locating so a new kind is added in one place.

Modal dialog scaffolding (native <dialog>, showModal/close $effect, backdrop-click-to-close, X header button, and the .{rename,move}-dialog + ::backdrop CSS block with surface-1/radius-lg/shadow-panel/blur(8px)) is duplicated verbatim with nav/MoveToSectionDialog.svelte. Extract one shared ModalDialog primitive to avoid drift in modal behavior and styling.

NsfwBlur, NsfwText, and NsfwTagLabel are three near-identical components implementing the same `!isNsfw || nsfw.mode === "show"` visibility gate, differing only by wrapper element (div vs span vs span-with-text-prop). This is copy-paste triplication of the same logic; consolidate into a single NsfwGate. NsfwBlur is additionally misnamed since it gates visibility rather than blurring.

nsfwMode is typed as a bare `string` (and threaded that way into helpers like displayJobHeading/maintenanceJobLogRedacted) even though a precise NsfwMode = "off" | "show" union already exists in $lib/nsfw/cookie. Typing it loosely defeats the closed-set guarantee and lets invalid mode strings flow through unchecked.

Maintains its own ordered list and label/icon/href map for entity kinds in parallel to entity-codes.ts (ENTITY_KIND, ENTITY_KIND_LABELS, ROUTE_RULES.browsePath). Labels ('Movies','People','Studios') duplicate ENTITY_KIND_LABELS and hrefs duplicate resolveEntityBrowsePath, so adding/renaming a kind requires edits in two places. Should derive from the canonical entity-codes module and only own the icon mapping.

Test asserts implementation details by grepping the .svelte/.app.css source for exact class names and font-size strings (e.g. 'font-size: 0.74rem !important', 'border-radius: 0;'). This is brittle: any cosmetic refactor of SubtitlesSection or app.css breaks the test without a behavior change, and it tests nothing the component actually does at runtime.

Optimistic-update + rollback logic is copy-pasted across handleToggleRoot, handleToggleMediaType, and handleToggleNsfw (set local state, await updateLibraryRoot, revert on error, invalidateAll). handleToggleNsfw also silently swallows errors (empty catch, no onError) unlike the others. The three should collapse into one generic patchRoot(root, patch) helper with consistent error handling.

Owns four near-identical raw control implementations (integer stepper, decimal range, text, stringList) plus a large <style> block restyling the range thumb/track. The integer/decimal variants duplicate NumberStepper.svelte and QualitySlider.svelte respectively, so number-control behavior (clamping, commit-on-blur, thumb styling) is implemented 3-4 times across the cluster with no shared primitive.

Logo-art detection hardcodes both an image-asset role literal ("logo") and an entity-kind literal ("studio") inline, bypassing the generated ENTITY_FILE_ROLE/ENTITY_KIND codes the rest of the entity layer (incl. this file's sibling universal-lightbox-media.ts) consistently references. Pure consumer-side drift risk if those codes change.

Section-id strings (description/links/tags/studio/credits/dates/stats/positions/classification/rating/flags) drive both validateDraft and buildMetadataUpdate via stringly-typed hasSection(). The two functions must stay in lockstep with the EditableSection ids produced elsewhere; with no shared const, adding/renaming a section silently breaks validation or patch building. Note also the order-dependent coupling: rating/flags are folded into the description section and only re-added standalone when description is absent (lines 244-255).

creditSubtitle decides visibility by string-comparing the humanized label "Person" (labelForCreditRole output) instead of the canonical CREDIT_ROLE.person code. The display-formatting function and the business rule are entangled, so a label tweak would change behavior.

Reader mode vocabulary (paged/webtoon) and progress-unit vocabulary (page/cfi) are duplicated here as bare literals while book-reader-route.ts independently declares the BookReaderMode union — two sources of truth for the same reader-mode concept with no shared runtime constant linking them.

Bitrate/popularity hiding logic is spread and partly re-implemented: entity-detail has HIDDEN_STAT_CODES + shouldDisplayStat (line 200-204), while entity-grid.ts independently filters stat codes containing bit-rate/bitrate (line 285) and has a separate isBitrateLabel regex (line 140) for the lightweight-thumbnail path. Three different mechanisms decide the same 'do not surface bitrate/popularity' policy across the detail and grid mappers; consolidate into one shared stat-code policy helper.

buildCapabilityFilterOptions is ~230 lines mixing static option seeding (addUniqueOption) with per-card capability scanning (a 130-line switch). The same family handling appears again in entityMatchesFilter (another large switch) and partially in entityGridFilterFromId. This is the layering hot-spot of the cluster: filter-option generation, id-resolution, and client matching for each capability family should be colocated per-family (a registry of FilterFamily handlers) rather than three parallel switches kept in sync by hand.

parseSubtitleSource and parseSubtitleSourceFormat are hand-rolled switch-validators that hardcode the full closed set of SubtitleSource/SubtitleSourceFormat values instead of driving validation from the canonical type definitions in $lib/player/subtitle-types. If a new source/format is added to the type, these parsers silently coerce it to the fallback ('manual'/'vtt') with no compile error. Same drift pattern the EnumCodec/codes.ts system exists to prevent. Centralize the allowed set and derive the parser from it.

codecLabel and sourceRangeLabel re-implement codec/range normalization by string comparison ('h264'/'avc'->'H.264', 'hevc'->'HEVC', VideoRangeType 'DOVI'/'SDR'). videoCodecBadge and dynamicRangeBadge are already imported from $lib/player/media-badges and used elsewhere in this same file (lines 115-116). The colorPipelineLabel helper duplicates that codec/range-to-label logic locally instead of reusing the shared badge helpers, risking divergent labels for the same codec.

Trickplay image asset is matched by asset.kind === ENTITY_FILE_ROLE.trickplay, but ENTITY_FILE_ROLE is the file-role vocabulary, not the image-asset-kind vocabulary. The supportedKinds in fixtures use 'trickplay'/'thumbnail' as image kinds (test line 110). Reusing a file-role constant as an image-asset-kind discriminator conflates two distinct closed sets that only happen to share the spelling 'trickplay'; they could drift independently.

reconcileFileTreeEntries reparses the loadedKeys composite key with `key.split(/:(.*)/s)` to recover the relative path, but the key format `${rootId}:${path}` is constructed inline elsewhere (e.g. unloadedExpandedDirectories line 136 and the routes/components that own loadedKeys). The `rootId:path` key encoding is an implicit contract duplicated across modules with no shared encode/decode helper, which is a drift/correctness risk if a rootId ever contains a colon.

jobTypeForQueue returns the FIRST definition whose queueName matches, but several queues map to multiple job types (e.g. metadata-import has import-metadata, auto-identify, bulk-identify; library-maintenance has library-maintenance and noop). For these queues jobTypeForQueue silently returns only one type, which is a latent correctness bug if callers use it to enqueue or filter by a representative type.

describeTrigger's switch over job.triggeredBy omits the 'audio-scan' case even though JobTriggerKind explicitly includes 'audio-scan'; audio-scan-triggered jobs fall through to the default 'Trigger not recorded' instead of an audio-specific message. Switch is not exhaustive over the union.

mapJobRun hardcodes triggeredBy:'system', triggerLabel:'Queued by jobs', and jobKind:'standard' for every job because the API JobRun DTO (generated model) carries none of those fields. The JobRun model in models.ts exposes triggeredBy/triggerLabel/jobKind and helpers.ts (describeTrigger/isForceRebuildJob/toneForJob) branch on them, so that entire trigger/force-rebuild presentation path is effectively dead for API-sourced jobs - the data shape and the consumer logic are out of sync.

The global keydown listener is registered inside `$effect.root(...)` in the class constructor but the root scope is never disposed, so the cleanup return (removeEventListener) is effectively never invoked for the lifetime of the store. `$effect.root` creates a non-tracked root that must be torn down explicitly; here the teardown is unreachable, meaning the listener leaks if the store is ever recreated (e.g. across context re-provides / HMR). The `keydownAttached` guard mitigates duplicates within one instance but does not free the prior root.

Cross-boundary coupling by convention only: the PRESETS table's rung names MUST match the server's JellyfinQualityPresetOptions (HlsAssetService.Encoding.cs) or a pinned tier resolves to a 404 variant playlist. This invariant is enforced only by a comment and tests, with no shared/generated source of truth between client and server (consistent with the player-quality-picker-client-ladder memory). Drift risk if the server preset table changes.

hlsStatusUrlForSrc encodes the streaming route topology ('/video-stream/{id}/hls2/master.m3u8' -> '/hls2/status', preserving query string) as a regex/string-replace in the frontend. This couples the player to the .NET stream route template with no shared contract; a server route rename silently 404s readiness checks. Centralize the hls2 path segments in a streaming-URL helper aligned with the server route.

The fetch-param translation re-validates filter values that validateXListPrefs already accepted only as opaque strings: rating range (1-5 int), date regex ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$, and true/false guards live in to-fetch-params. This validation logic is duplicated verbatim between series and videos (rating clamp, date regex, organized true/false) and would also be the natural home for the shared FILTER_TYPE table.

The person<->performer alias is encoded twice in opposite directions (toSearchKind forward, toResultGroup reverse) as inline bare literals. Two independent conditionals must stay in sync by hand; the alias should be one shared mapping/constant so the forward and reverse translations cannot diverge.

toSearchKind does a runtime membership check by casting ALL_SEARCH_KINDS to string[] and comparing the raw EntityCard.kind string, then casting the result back to SearchEntityKind. This bypasses type safety at the API boundary; if the backend introduces a new kind code it is silently dropped (returns null) with no telemetry, and the cast (kind as SearchEntityKind) is unchecked. A code-registry-backed lookup would make unmapped kinds explicit.

valuesToLibrarySettings re-implements normalization that already exists: it inlines `playbackDefaultMode === "hls" ? "hls" : "direct"` (line 228) and `visibilityDefaultMode === "show" ? "show" : "off"` (line 151) instead of using the normalizePlaybackMode helper in library-settings.ts. Two divergent code paths normalize the same playback-mode value, and visibility has no shared normalizer at all.

defaultLibrarySettings hardcodes a large set of default values (entity kinds, playback mode, subtitle style, transcoder profile, language strings) that the backend AppSettings catalog also defines as defaults. This frontend default table can drift from the server's authoritative descriptor defaults; the per-field literals here duplicate canonical codes rather than referencing them.

uploadFiles() has four near-identical root/library resolution branches (video/image/audio/book): each calls a loadXRoots helper, returns on null, auto-resolves when length===1, otherwise stashes pendingFiles + sets candidates. This is copy-paste with only the loader and the candidate field differing — collapses to a single table-driven flow keyed by target.kind.

loadVideoRoots / loadImageRoots / loadBookRoots are byte-for-byte identical except the query string (scanVideos/scanImages/scanBooks) and the empty-state message; loadAudioLibraries differs only in response shape ({items} vs {roots}) and endpoint. Four methods that should be one parameterized loader (path + emptyMessage).

The book-with-bookId and image-with-galleryId targets fall through to the final runUploads(files) with no explicit destination, relying entirely on uploadOne() branch order to route correctly. The control flow is implicit (no kind check at line 99); a target whose kind isn't handled in uploadOne would silently hit the audio fallback branch (line 279-282) rather than erroring.

readNsfwCookie() reimplements nsfw-cookie reading (regex + parse) inside the layout, duplicating the cookie-name and read logic that already lives in $lib/nsfw/store.svelte.ts / $lib/utils/cookie. The layout then double-reads the cookie at module init (defaultLayoutData) and again in three provideNsfw callbacks (lines 36-37, 48, 50), so the cookie is parsed 5+ times and the canonical cookie name is forked. Should call a shared reader from the nsfw module.

SECTION_DEFS is a hand-maintained parallel list of (kind,label,icon,href) that re-encodes per-kind labels and browse paths already owned by entity-codes (ENTITY_KIND_LABELS, ROUTE_RULES[].browsePath). The dashboard, the route table, and the label map can drift independently; the section set should be derived from the canonical kind registry plus an icon map rather than redeclared.

EntityIndexPage declares `kind: string` rather than `EntityKindCode`. This loose typing is why every index route (artists/movies/comics/ebooks and siblings) passes a bare string literal with no compile-time guarantee it is a real entity kind. Tightening the prop to EntityKindCode (from entity-codes) would force routes to reference ENTITY_KIND.* and catch typos.

Test asserts an exact source substring of the .svelte file (lockedServerQuery + lockBookFilters). Same anti-pattern as jobs/worker-status-style.test.ts which pins inline CSS text. These tests break on harmless formatting/refactor and don't verify behavior; they also encode the un-centralized BookType/BookFormat codes a third time. Prefer behavioral assertions or asserting against shared constants.

Duplication between identify/+page.svelte and identify/[entityId]/+page.svelte: both build the error boundary fallback card, the inline alert rows, and the review-* view switch (review-child/parent/choice) by hand. The view-dispatch + fallback should live in one shared component so the two entry points stay in sync (currently the [entityId] page adds an `ancestors` prop the dashboard page omits — divergence already started).

File operations use native prompt()/alert()/confirm() (createFolder, renameFile, moveFile, deleteFile, exclude). These bypass the Prism Noir design system entirely (browser-chrome dialogs), are untestable, and block the event loop. Should route through an app modal/confirm primitive.

Filter UI is partly dead/non-functional: dateFrom/dateTo and the loadMore() handler are wired up in the template but explicitly voided ('void rating; void from; void to' L109-111; loadMore is a documented no-op L156-159). minRating is the only filter actually applied (client-side in filterSearchResponse). Date filters render and update the URL effect but never affect results — a correctness/UX gap.

Filtering is done client-side after fetching up to directLimit:160 results, then sliced to PAGE_SIZE 20 per group (filterSearchResponse). Rating/kind filtering and pagination happen in the browser rather than server-side, so totals shown ('Show more (N remaining)') are based only on the fetched window, not the true library total — counts can be misleading.

Very large route component (~860 lines) mixing playback-progress reporting (Jellyfin session ticks), subtitle localStorage hydration, transcript-dock resize math, viewport media-query handling, and entity mutations. Much of the player/transcript plumbing is shared conceptually with videos/[id] (it imports video-page-state and VideoDetailSectionContent from there). Consider extracting a shared player-page controller hook so movie and video detail don't re-implement progress/subtitle/dock state.

Settings page hardcodes which setting keys belong to which visual group (subtitleBehaviorKeys/subtitleAppearanceKeys L100-106, generationControls aggregating scan+taxonomy+generation+jobs groups L148-155). This grouping logic lives in the route rather than being driven by setting descriptor metadata from the catalog, so adding a setting requires editing this page's literal key lists.

Heavy structural duplication between the two audio detail routes: near-identical load-state machine (LoadState type, loading/error/ready, lastNsfwMode effect, redirectHiddenEntityNotFound, retry), identical handleRatingChange/handleFavoriteToggle/handleOrganizedToggle/handleMetadataSave handlers, identical error-notice and heroMeta markup + CSS. Much of this is also shared with non-audio detail routes. A shared entity-detail page controller (load + nsfw-reload + optimistic flag/rating/metadata handlers) plus shared error/meta primitives would remove the copy-paste.

handleTrackRename builds a full EntityMetadataUpdateRequest patch by hand, hardcoding every field to its empty value (description: null, externalIds: {}, urls: [], tags: [], studio: null, credits: [], dates: {}, stats: {}, positions: {}, classification: null) just to change the title. This couples the route to the exact metadata DTO shape; any added field silently won't be cleared/passed. A shared helper to build a single-field metadata patch (or a partial-patch API) would be safer and less brittle.

loadVolume computes nextProgress via bookEntityProgressDisplay(...) but never assigns or uses the result — dead work (an eagerly-built progress display that is discarded). The reactive `progressDisplay`/`volumeProgress` deriveds already recompute it, so this in-loader call is purely redundant and should be removed.

ReaderMode ("paged"|"webtoon"), ReaderFlow ("paginated"|"scrolled"), and the progress unit/mode vocab are re-declared as local types in the reader route while equivalent unions already exist in book-reader-route.ts (BookReaderMode), book-entity-reader.ts, BookFileReader.svelte and PdfReader.svelte. These parallel definitions are the layering smell behind the magic-string drift: the reader 'flow'/'mode'/'unit' vocabulary has no single owner. Centralize the types + value constants and import them.

Test relies entirely on raw source-text string matching (readFile + source.toContain) of .svelte files rather than rendering/behavioral assertions. It asserts on literal substrings like 'value: "video-series"', 'icon: SlidersHorizontal', 'standaloneMetadataSectionIds={[]}', and 'kind: "image-sequence"'. This is brittle: any rename, formatting/whitespace change, or refactor that preserves behavior will break it, and it can pass even when the component is broken. It also reaches across package boundaries (reads ../../packages/contracts/src/collections.ts) coupling the route test to contract file text.

Collection mode/coverMode are compared with bare string literals ("dynamic"/"hybrid"/"item") even though CollectionMode and CollectionCoverMode typed unions exist in src/lib/collections/models.ts. Because those are type-only unions with no value-constant counterpart, every consumer must hardcode the strings — drift risk if a mode is renamed. Recommend adding value consts (e.g. COLLECTION_MODE / COLLECTION_COVER_MODE) alongside the unions and referencing them here.

All four tests in this cluster (gallery-page-navigation, gallery-detail-layout, gallery-detail-lightbox) are source-text assertion tests: they readFileSync the .svelte file and toContain('const currentGalleryId = $derived(...)'), match the .detail-page CSS rule, and assert on substrings like 'lightboxCards = nextCards' and 'prefsKey={`gallery-${gallery?.id}-images`}'. They assert on implementation source rather than rendered behavior, so they are extremely brittle - any rename/reformat (e.g. changing quoting, refactoring loadGallery, or extracting the content-section markup recommended above) breaks them without a behavior change, and they give no real regression coverage of navigation/layout/lightbox behavior.

Dead/placeholder code: `studio` is `$derived.by(() => null)` (line 63) and `credits` is `$derived.by(() => [])` (line 65), so the heroMeta studio link (line 161-163) and the entire afterBody credits-section (lines 172-187) are unreachable. This drags in unused imports (Users, resolveEntityHref) and ~30 lines of CSS for chips that never render. Either wire these to real image capabilities or delete the scaffolding.

Tests assert against raw source-file text (readFile + toContain/not.toContain on '<UniversalLightbox', '{#snippet detailsContent()}', 'showHero={false}', etc.). These are brittle structural snapshots, not behavioral tests — any benign refactor (whitespace, snippet rename, attribute reorder) breaks them without indicating a real regression. Prefer rendering the component and asserting behavior.

Same brittle source-string-assertion anti-pattern: the test reads BOTH +page.svelte and the shared EntityIndexPage.svelte and greps for substrings like 'ImageLightboxDetails', '{#snippet detailsContent(entity', 'hydrateLightboxEntity'. It couples the images route test to internal implementation strings of a shared component, so unrelated refactors of EntityIndexPage will fail this image-route test.

The `prismedia:` auth-key namespace and `prismedia:${id}:${field.key}` value-key are constructed in +page.svelte and independently re-parsed/re-constructed in InstalledPluginsTab.svelte (253,355,359) and PrismediaCommunityTab.svelte (249,254). This cross-file string convention has no single owner; a shared helper (e.g. providerAuthKey/providerAuthValueKey) should produce and match these keys.

Scraper capability handling is split across three representations: CAPABILITY_META label map (30-56), direct caps.sceneByURL/... boolean reads in filteredScrapers (133-135), and enabledCaps generic Object.entries. The capability vocabulary is duplicated between the map keys and the filter predicate, so adding a capability requires edits in multiple places with no shared constant to keep them aligned.

matchesProviderCapabilityFilter hardcodes entity-kind codes "video"/"video-series" (line 164) instead of ENTITY_KIND constants, and the CapFilter type allows "performer" but the provider branch always returns false for it (line 168) — providers are silently filtered out under the Performer chip, an inconsistency vs the scraper filter.

Pagination helpers (getSeriesLoadedWindow, seriesPageHref, applySeriesPageMerge) appear to be dead code: no .svelte file in the series routes imports them — only series-page-pagination.test.ts references them. The actual series index uses EntityIndexPage/EntityGrid which own their own paging. Either wire these into the page or remove them; tested-but-unused helpers give false confidence.

Selection helpers (toggleSelectedId, getSelectionState, selectAllVisibleIds) are likewise only referenced by tests, not by any series .svelte route (which pass selectable={false} to EntityGrid). Dead code with passing tests; remove or wire in.

Tests assert on raw source text via regex (readFileSync of the .svelte file + string .toContain / readCssRule regex). This couples tests to incidental formatting — e.g. it asserts source contains exact strings like 'sections: ["description", "tags", "cast-and-crew"]' and CSS 'padding: 0;'. A whitespace/reorder change breaks tests without a behavior change, and the tests can't catch runtime regressions. Prefer rendering the component and asserting behavior/DOM over grepping source.

loadSeasonEpisodeCounts fan-outs a fetchSeason call per season id (Promise.all over every season) purely to compute episode totals for a hero label, in addition to fetchOrderedEntityThumbnails for the same seasons. For a series with many seasons this is an N+1 fetch on every page load / nsfw toggle; consider returning season episode counts from the series detail payload instead.

This route component is very large (~915 lines) and mixes many concerns: data loading, series-chain resolution, movie-child redirect, Jellyfin progress reporting + interval timer, subtitle localStorage hydration, transcript dock state + pointer-driven resizing, and entity mutations. Several effects manage interdependent mutable flags (playTracked, resumeApplied, trackedVideoId, hydratedSubtitlePrefsKey). The pure helpers were partially extracted to video-page-state.ts; the playback-session tracking and transcript-dock resize logic are good candidates to extract into composables (.svelte.ts) to shrink and test the component.

Subtitle-language and transcript-dock localStorage keys are split across two ownership sites: transcript-dock keys live in video-page-state.ts (constants + typed read/write helpers) but the 'prismedia:subtitle-lang:' key and its '__off__' sentinel are inlined twice directly in +page.svelte. Inconsistent: the subtitle-language persistence should follow the same helper pattern (key builder + parse/serialize) in video-page-state.ts to avoid drift between the read and write sites.

The test builds LibrarySettings with subtitleStyle: 'native' and asserts it round-trips through buildSubtitleDefaults, but 'native' is not a member of SubtitleDisplayStyle (subtitle-types.ts = 'stylized'|'classic'|'outline') nor of the generated SUBTITLE_STYLE const (only 'stylized'). buildSubtitleDefaults blind-casts the value `as SubtitleDisplayStyle`, so an invalid style passes through untyped. The cast hides the type error and the test encodes a value the rest of the system doesn't recognize — correctness/drift risk.

This suite asserts implementation details by string-matching component source (e.g. expect(pageSource).toContain('id: "details"'), 'showHero={false}', 'class="flex-1 p-5"', specific CSS rule bodies). These tests are brittle: harmless refactors (renaming a section id, reformatting, switching to a constant for the kind/section ids) will break them without any behavior change, and they pin literals that the magic-string cleanup above would want to replace with constants. Prefer rendering-based or DOM assertions over source-substring matching.

Section ids ('cast-and-crew','technical','dates','playback','source','markers','transcript') are defined in +page.svelte's detailSections/detailTabs descriptors and re-matched as string literals in VideoDetailSectionContent's #if chain, with no shared constant binding the producer and consumer. A rename in one place silently breaks the switch. These section ids are a closed set local to the video detail page and should be a shared const map (or enum) referenced by both files.

API_BASE_URL reads process.env.PUBLIC_API_URL/API_URL at runtime in a package that is also imported into Svelte browser code; relying on process.env in the browser is fragile (depends on bundler define/replacement) and the hardcoded http://localhost:8008/api fallback couples the contract package to the dev port. Consider moving base-URL resolution into the app layer.

1131-line god-file holding unrelated DTO families (video series, galleries, books, images, jobs, scrapers, plugins, stash-box, performers, studios, tags, audio, scenes, collections re-export). The package already splits by concern (collections.ts, library.ts, queries.ts, subtitles.ts) so these should be split into matching per-domain modules; many of these DTOs also duplicate the OpenAPI-generated client types.

typeCounts/byType are typed Record<CollectionEntityType, number> but CollectionEntityType (collections.ts:3) and the EntityKind union (frontend-dtos.ts:672) are two different hand-maintained closed sets with overlapping but non-identical members ('movie' present in both, but performer/studio/tag etc. only in EntityKind). Single canonical EntityKindCode should drive both to prevent the keys from drifting apart.

CollectionItemDto.entity is typed Record<string,unknown>|null and ScrapeResultDto.rawResult/proposedResult/proposedSeriesResult/proposedAudioResult are 'unknown' — polymorphic embeds discriminated only by an entityType/kind string at runtime. This pushes type-narrowing into every consumer and is the kind of untyped ad-hoc shape CLAUDE.md's quality bar warns against; consider a discriminated union keyed off the canonical entity-kind code.

The canonical generated-asset cache layout (videos/<id>/ subdir + filenames preview.mp4 / sprite.jpg / trickplay.vtt / thumb / card) is duplicated cross-language between this TS module and the backend C# AssetPathService.cs and MaintenancePersistenceService.cs (videos/<id>/preview.mp4, sprite.jpg, trickplay.vtt). There is no shared source of truth, so the two sides can drift silently.

Three near-identical ffprobe-spawn-and-map functions (probeVideoFile/probeImageFile/probeAudioFile) duplicate the '-v error -of json' invocation shape and container-from-extension fallback logic (formatName ?? extContainer). Only probeVideoFile wraps errors in CorruptMediaError; probeImageFile swallows all errors and probeAudioFile lets them propagate raw — inconsistent error handling across the three probes.

This whole package (parsing/probe/subtitles/sorting) has no TypeScript consumers in the repo — grep finds no importers outside media-core itself and its tests. Per the architecture contract scan/probe/subtitle work runs in the .NET worker, so this media-core TS package appears to be parallel/legacy logic that duplicates backend responsibilities (subtitle format vocab, ffprobe wrappers, filename parsing) without being wired into the running app. Confirm whether it is still live.

executeStashScraper claims to handle performer actions but pluginInputToPerformerFragment builds a fragment, calls scrapePerformer, then unconditionally returns null (line 115) and discards the result — `result` is computed and thrown away. Either the performer branch is dead/incomplete or the scrapePerformer call is a pointless side-effect; the eslint 'unused result' is suppressed by the discard. Worth resolving (remove the call or actually convert/return the performer result).

prismediaToStashActionMap omits image/audio/movie/series/episode/folder capability keys, so the Stash adapter silently can only ever serve video+gallery+book+performer actions; any other capability resolves to 'No Stash action mapping' at runtime. The map is also a partial mirror of the stash-compat action vocabulary maintained by hand (drift risk against packages/stash-compat/src/types.ts).

indexCache is module-level mutable state keyed only by TTL, not by indexUrl. If two different registry URLs are fetched within the 5-minute window, the second call returns the first URL's cached entries (resolved URL is ignored on cache hit). Cache should be keyed by resolved index URL.

deduplicateNames is implemented identically (byte-for-byte) in both normalizers — community-scraper normalizer.ts:89-103 and stashbox/normalizer.ts:142-154. Two copies of the same case-insensitive-dedup helper that should live in one shared util in the package.

Tattoo/piercing formatting ([location, description].filter(Boolean).join(': ') / join('; ')) and the measurements/aliases formatting are implemented twice — once in normalizeStashBoxPerformer (lines 49-57,77) and again inline in stashBoxSceneToRawResult's performers map (lines 127-133,126). The two paths can drift in how a performer is rendered.

The set of Stash capability keys (sceneByURL...groupByURL) is maintained as three independent hand-written lists: capabilityKeys here, the capability array in packages/plugins/src/types.ts, and SupportedCapabilities in apps/backend StashScraperDefinition.cs. No single source; adding a capability requires editing all three or they silently disagree.

Regex post-process rules are compiled with new RegExp(rule.regex, "s") per-value with no error handling — a malformed regex in an untrusted community scraper YAML throws a raw SyntaxError that is not wrapped in ScraperExecutionError (it would surface only via the outer try/catch in runXPathScraper for evaluate, but buildQueryURL at line 165 runs before that try block, so a bad queryURLReplace regex throws unwrapped). Correctness/robustness gap against hostile scraper definitions.

resolveActionDef indexes definition[action as keyof ScraperYamlDef] with a caller-supplied `action: string` and casts to keyof — the prototype-pollution guard used elsewhere (getOwnString/hasOwnField) is not applied here, so an inherited property named like an action could be picked up. The yaml-parser.test.ts only covers parseScraperYaml's pollution safety, not resolveActionDef's lookup.

evaluateSubObject returns only { name, url } (drops every other selected key), while evaluateSubObjectArray returns only { name } — so studio image/parent and performer metadata defined in an xPathScrapers sub-object are silently discarded even though StashScrapedStudio/Performer model those fields. The XPath engine is materially lossier than the script engine it sits beside.

StatusLed is a shared primitive but multiple consumers (apps/web-svelte Sidebar.svelte:424, MobileMoreSheet.svelte:439, settings/WatchedLibrariesSection.svelte:371, and even sibling primitive Toggle.svelte:80) bypass it and hardcode <span class="led led-active|led-idle">. The led-* class names are an undocumented utility contract (defined in app.css) duplicated across files; these should render <StatusLed status=...> instead so the status->class mapping has a single owner.

OpenArchiveEntryAsync fully buffers the decompressed zip entry into a MemoryStream and then serves it with enableRangeProcessing:true. For large book/comic archive members this loads the entire member into memory per request and range requests still pay full decompression, which is a scalability/correctness concern for big files.

ffmpeg transcode parameters (libmp3lame, 192k, 44100, 2ch, mp3) and the 'no-store' cache policy are hardcoded in an API-layer IResult. ffmpeg orchestration is otherwise a worker/infrastructure concern; embedding a transcode pipeline directly in the Api project blurs the Clean Architecture boundary and makes the audio transcode profile untestable/unshared with the rest of the ffmpeg code.

Client operation names are derived by string-munging detailName ('Get' prefix stripped to build Create*/Delete*). This relies on every caller naming its detail op 'GetX' and breaks silently if a caller deviates; passing an explicit base/operation name would be safer than the inline prefix heuristic.

StreamContent opens the file with File.OpenRead(content.AbsolutePath) and hands the FileStream to Results.File without an existence/IO guard at this layer; any race or stale path between GetContentInfoAsync and OpenRead surfaces as an unmapped 500 rather than the ApiProblem path used elsewhere in this file. Minor robustness gap in an otherwise consistently error-mapped endpoint set.

Upload calls NsfwVisibility.ShouldHide(null, ...) — it never reads a hideNsfw form/query value, so the NSFW visibility decision for uploads is hardcoded to the header/context default while every other Files endpoint accepts an explicit bool? hideNsfw. Inconsistent visibility handling across the Files surface; intentional or an oversight worth confirming.

Health response status "ok"/"online"/"offline" and provider "dotnet" are inline literals whose frontend mirror (prismedia.ts WorkerHealthResponse.status union) is maintained by hand; not covered by the generated codes pipeline. Low risk but a small drift surface between backend status producers and the frontend union type.

Two near-identical ToUserDto overloads (JellyfinProfileResponse at lines 54-67 and JellyfinProfile at 69-82) duplicate the entire 14-field DTO construction with only the source-type differing. Drift risk: a field added to one will silently diverge. Consider mapping both source types to a shared shape or a single private builder.

GetLatest/GetResume/GetNextUp each independently re-read StartIndex/Limit from httpContext.Request.Query and re-apply Math.Clamp(..,1,100)/Math.Max(0,..) instead of going through ItemQueryFrom, duplicating both the query-key strings and the clamping bounds across .Catalog.cs lines 102-141. A shared paging-parse helper would remove the divergence.

"Prismedia" product/server name is hardcoded at ~7 sites (System/Ping text, ToPublicSystemInfo product+server names, ToUserDto ServerName). Borderline display text, but it functions as a stable client-facing server identity scalar repeated across handlers; a single ProductName constant would prevent drift.

StreamItemFileAsync uses File.OpenRead on a service-provided absolute path and serves it directly; same direct File.OpenRead pattern repeats in JellyfinPlaybackResults (StreamVideo/HLS/trickplay) and StreamImageAsync. The path-trust boundary lives in the services, but the raw filesystem access is spread across many endpoint handlers with no shared file-result helper.

Delete responses use ad-hoc anonymous object shapes (`new { ok = true }` here, `new { killed }` in VideoTranscodeEndpoints.cs:11) rather than a typed contract DTO. These untyped boundary shapes contradict the repo's typed-contract rule and don't flow cleanly into the generated OpenAPI/Svelte client; prefer a small typed response record.

A new HttpClient (wrapping a SocketsHttpHandler) is constructed inside UseSpaDevServer and captured by the middleware closure. This is dev-only and registered once at startup so it does not socket-leak per request, but it lives for the app lifetime without going through IHttpClientFactory. Acceptable for a dev proxy; worth a comment that it is intentionally a singleton-for-app-lifetime client.

Response header forwarding copies all response + content headers then explicitly removes only 'transfer-encoding'. Other hop-by-hop headers (Connection, Keep-Alive, etc.) are not stripped. For a dev-only Vite proxy this is low risk, but it is a minor correctness gap in header relaying.
No issues — clean, correct generic converter factory that defers entirely to CodecRegistry. This is the canonical wire-serialization for [Code] enums and contains no hardcoded codes.

ManualMembershipAsync rebuilds a full Collection aggregate from just a mode (with a dummy 'Collection' title and EmptyRuleJson) solely to read CanEditManualMembership. This couples the command service to the aggregate constructor signature for a single boolean; a mode-level rule (CollectionMode.CanEditManualMembership or a domain helper) would be cleaner and avoid fabricating throwaway aggregates.

Type leak/inconsistency: CollectionVisibleRuleMatch and PreviewRulesAsync key on EntityType as a raw string, while AddItemsAsync (CollectionCommandService.cs:80) resolves EntityType through EntityKindRegistry.TryGet into a typed EntityKind. The visible-match path keeps an untyped string code that is grouped with StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase (CollectionCommandService.cs:152) and never validated against the registry, so an unknown/casing-variant entity-type code flows straight into the preview response counts.

Doc-comment says DeleteAsync 'Soft-deletes a collection entity' (also ICollectionCommandPersistence.cs:15 'Soft-deletes'), but project memory records a hard-delete-only model (no soft delete / DeletedAt; deletes rely on FK cascade). Either the comments are stale or the implementation diverges from the stated invariant; worth reconciling.

DeterministicId/EtagFor use MD5/SHA1 (System.Security.Cryptography) purely for stable id/etag derivation. Correctness-wise fine (not security), but worth noting these are non-cryptographic uses of crypto hashes; a dedicated stable-hash helper would document intent and avoid future security-scanner noise.

Bare 'video' literal for TargetEntityKind on the self-enqueued ProbeVideo request diverges from the established pattern (RefreshEntityJobHandler/AutoIdentifyScanEnqueue pass entity.KindCode / EntityKind code), creating drift risk if the video kind code ever changes. Minor: the 5x5 trickplay grid and 320/160 dimension bounds are inline magic numbers but those are tuning constants, not identifiers.

ReportProgressAsync computes (i+1)*100/count with no guard for count==0; an empty EntityIds payload would divide by zero. Low likelihood (callers presumably gate on non-empty) but the handler does not defend against it.

JobContext exposes a public constructor taking IJobQueueService (an application port). Minor: it is documented as the handler execution context but is freely constructable; fine for tests but worth confirming only the worker dispatcher builds it. No correctness bug, noting for boundary clarity.

TargetKind = "library-root" is a pseudo entity-kind used only on root-scoped scan jobs. It is NOT a member of the EntityKind [Code] set, yet it occupies the same TargetEntityKind field that elsewhere carries real EntityKind codes ("video", etc.). The literal is duplicated as documentation-only mentions across JobRunRow.cs:24, JobContracts.cs:11, and EnqueueJobRequest.cs:10. This constant is the de-facto source of truth and is referenced correctly, but the design mixes a non-entity sentinel into the entity-kind code space, which weakens the closed-set guarantee EntityKindRegistry provides. Consider modeling library-root as a real EntityKindMeta-tagged code or a distinct target-kind enum.

RebuildPreviewsAsync and BackfillFingerprintsAsync (lines 130-135) each hand-roll a (EntityKind, JobType) tuple array mapping entity kinds to their generation/fingerprint job types. This kind->jobtype mapping is the same kind of dispatch knowledge encoded in LibraryScanJobs.ScanJobTypesFor and likely duplicated in worker handlers. Two separate inline tuple tables in one file is a small drift risk; a single shared mapping (e.g. on EntityKindMeta or a JobType-for-kind helper) would keep preview/fingerprint/scan job selection consistent.

IdentifyCascadePayload owns its own JsonSerializer.Serialize/Deserialize (ToJson/Parse) with default options. Several job payloads across the app likely each define ad-hoc serialization; without a shared serializer-options instance, casing/enum-handling conventions can diverge between payload types and between producer (API) and consumer (worker). Consider a shared job-payload serialization helper with fixed JsonSerializerOptions.

The scheduler tracks scanVideos/scanImages/scanAudio/scanBooks as four parallel bool locals threaded through ScanJobTypesFor and QueueScansForKindsAsync. This 4-bool fan-out is repeated in LibraryScanJobs (ScanJobTypesFor and QueueScansForKindsAsync both take the same four bools). Adding a new scannable kind requires editing every signature. A small per-kind flags struct or kind set would localize that growth, though current correctness is fine.

EntityRefreshTarget.KindCode (and AutoIdentifyRootTarget.KindCode line 151, OrganizeEntityRow.KindCode in OrganizeModels.cs) are 'string' kind codes. Consumers resolve them correctly via EntityKindRegistry.TryGet, but RefreshEntityJobHandler switches on the raw KindCode against inline literals ("video","image","audio-track","book-page"). The projection contract would be safer typed as EntityKind to prevent the consumer-side literal switch from drifting; at minimum the carrier records are the layer where a typed kind could be enforced.

VideoUpsertItem XML doc has a duplicated <param name="Movie"> tag (lines 295 and 300) and the doc order does not match the record's positional parameter order. Minor doc-correctness issue that will produce duplicate-param compiler/doc warnings.

IBookFileMetadataReader and IBookCoverImageExtractor reference Prismedia.Domain.Entities.BookFormat via fully-qualified names inline rather than a using directive (the rest of the file's records do not import Domain). Cosmetic inconsistency; a 'using Prismedia.Domain.Entities;' would match LibraryScanPorts.cs's convention.

ComicInfoMetadata is reused as the normalized DTO for EPUB/PDF single-file book metadata (IBookFileMetadataReader.ReadAsync returns ComicInfoMetadata). The shape is named after one source format but serves as the generic book-metadata carrier, which is a semantic mismatch — a neutrally-named BookMetadata record would better reflect that it is provider-agnostic.

OrganizePlanItem.Status is a plain string driven by private consts here; the contract exposes it untyped. Correctness-wise the ApplyAsync ancestor-skip logic (lines 47-71) and status branching all hinge on exact string equality with no shared/typed status code, so the organize status vocabulary is effectively private to this class while also crossing the API boundary as bare strings.

GetCatalogAsync derives group metadata via AppSettingsRegistry.Definitions.First(d => d.GroupKey == group.Key) inside a per-group Select, an O(groups x definitions) scan and a fragile assumption that all definitions in a group carry identical GroupLabel/GroupDescription/GroupOrder. Group identity is modeled redundantly on every SettingDefinition rather than as a first-class group registry, allowing inconsistent group metadata to silently win by first-occurrence.

audioCopied is decided purely by string-equality on selectedAudioStream.Codec=="aac", but the remux pipeline's actual copy-vs-transcode decision lives in Infrastructure. If the remux logic changes which codecs it copies, IsAudioDirect advertised here becomes wrong with no shared source of truth.

AllowsDirectPlayback compares range.VideoRangeType against the bare literal "SDR" instead of VideoPlaybackRange.Sdr.VideoRangeType, even though the canonical Sdr instance is declared in this same file (line 103). The literal can drift from the record's actual value if the SDR spelling/casing ever changes. Reference the static field.

RequiresDolbyVisionToneMapping encodes Dolby Vision domain knowledge as raw magic numbers (dvProfile is 5, dvBlSignalCompatibilityId is 0). These DV profile/compatibility-id numeric codes are also reasoned about in VideoDirectPlayPolicy and (per doc) the tone-map filter selection. They are correct and centralized here, but the bare 5/0 would read better as named constants (e.g. DolbyVisionProfile.Profile5, DvBlSignalCompatibility.None) to document intent and prevent the numbers being re-typed elsewhere.

Contracts EntityDate(Code,Value,SortableValue,Precision) is a verbatim duplicate of Domain Prismedia.Domain/Capabilities/Shared/EntityDate.cs (same params, same XML doc). Two parallel record definitions for the same concept (Contracts DTO vs Domain model) require a hand-written mapper to stay in sync; field/precision drift between them is silent. Consider documenting/centralizing the mapping or generating one from the other.

EntitySubtitle.Format and SourceFormat are free string codes (e.g. 'vtt', 'srt') with NO canonical [Code] enum anywhere in Domain, unlike Source which maps to EntitySubtitleSource. Subtitle container formats are an unmodeled closed set, so producers/consumers risk drifting on casing/spelling. Add a SubtitleFormat [Code] enum and route Format/SourceFormat through it.

EntityDate.Code (release/air/birth/career-start) and Precision (year/month/day/text) are documented as 'stable codes' but have no [Code] enum in Domain, so there is no compile-time source of truth and the only documentation is the XML comment examples. These closed sets should be modeled as [Code] enums and exposed via codegen like other identifiers.

CollectionItemReference / CollectionItemDetail / CollectionRulePreviewItem all carry EntityType as a bare string entity-kind code at the API boundary. Canonical EntityKind [Code] enum + EntityKindRegistry exist; these DTOs intentionally degrade to string at the wire. Acceptable for the contract, but worth validating inbound EntityType against EntityKindRegistry in the handler so unknown kind codes are rejected rather than silently stored.

Kind is modeled as a plain string across the entire card/detail contract hierarchy (IEntityCard, EntityDetail, EntityThumbnail, FileLinkedEntity, EntityGroup, EntityKindCount). This is the contract-layer string-on-the-wire convention, but it means every consumer must reference the generated ENTITY_KIND/codes rather than a typed field; correctness depends on producers always sourcing Kind from EntityKind's EnumCodec. Worth a contract-wide note that these strings are codec-derived, not free-form.

JellyfinLibraryOptionsDto exposes PathInfos and TypeOptions as IReadOnlyList<object> (untyped). This defeats the typed-contract quality bar (CLAUDE.md: prefer typed contracts over ad hoc object shapes) and pushes shape knowledge into serialization/consumers; if these are intentionally always-empty stubs that should be documented, otherwise they should get real element types.

BookType and Format are typed as raw string in the contract even though closed domain enums BookType ([Code] book/comic/manga/novel) and BookFormat ([Code] image-archive/epub/pdf) exist. The wire code is correct to be a string scalar, but there is no documented invariant tying these to the canonical code set, so a typo or stale value can silently flow through the DTO. Consider documenting (and validating at the mapping boundary) that these carry the EnumCodec codes for BookType/BookFormat.

GalleryType is a raw string mirroring the domain GalleryType [Code] enum (virtual/folder/zip) with no stated linkage to the canonical code set, same drift risk as BookDetail. Worth a doc/validation note that this is the GalleryType code.

JobRun.Type/Status and JobQueueCountDto.Type/Status are untyped strings whose canonical values come from JobType (and a job-status enum). The contract relies entirely on producers using EnumCodec; there's no compile-time guarantee the dashboard and producers agree. Acceptable for a wire DTO, but the only safeguard is convention — flagging as a layering/typing observation, not a literal in this file.

JellyfinCatalogMediaStreamDto.Type (line 107, required string) and JellyfinCatalogMediaSourceDto fields carry Jellyfin stream-type scalars (Video/Audio/Subtitle) that already have constants in JellyfinProtocol.MediaTypes. The DTO correctly keeps them as strings for the wire, but the only spot in this cluster where defaults are inline-literal'd (Protocol='File', Type='Default') bypasses the JellyfinProtocol centralization the codebase otherwise enforces — see magicStrings. Producers populating Type should use JellyfinProtocol.MediaTypes.*.

JellyfinMediaSegmentDto is a defined-but-unproduced placeholder (per its own doc comment Prismedia does not yet produce segments). Harmless, but worth tracking so it doesn't rot; its Type field would also be a Jellyfin wire scalar that should map to a JellyfinProtocol constant set if/when segments are produced.

OrganizePlanItem.Kind and StorageShape are bare strings but are in fact EntityKind codes and EntityStorageShape codes (producer calls entity.KindCode and storageShape.ToCode() in OrganizeService.NewItem). The canonical homes are EntityKind [Code] and EntityStorageShape — the DTO loses that typing at the boundary. Acceptable as a wire DTO, but worth noting the field should be documented as referencing the EntityKind/StorageShape code vocabulary so frontend maps to ENTITY_KIND from codes.ts.

WorkerHealthResponse.Status is a bare string with an online/offline closed set documented only in the comment. Same pattern as the other System health/update DTOs: no shared status code, so producer and Svelte consumer independently hardcode 'online'/'offline'. Candidate for a small shared status enum/code.

PlaybackInfoResponse.ErrorCode is a Jellyfin PlaybackErrorCode wire scalar typed as nullable string with no documented vocabulary or shared constant. Strict clients (Infuse) decode this into an enum; values should be centralized in JellyfinProtocol rather than produced ad hoc.

JellyfinProfileResponse/Create/Update model a Jellyfin-compatible fake user as a first-class persisted concept in the Security contracts. This is fine, but note it overlaps the Jellyfin compatibility surface (JellyfinProtocol/Jellyfin contracts namespace) while living under Security; the layering of 'fake Jellyfin user' between the Security domain and the Jellyfin-compat surface is worth a deliberate boundary decision rather than splitting Jellyfin concepts across two namespaces.

SettingDescriptor.Key/GroupKey are setting-key identifiers and Type/InputKind/ApplyHint are closed UI-control codes, all typed as bare strings. Setting keys have a canonical home (AppSettingKeys in the Application layer, surfaced as SETTING_KEYS in codes.ts). The catalog DTO carries them untyped, so the curated settings UI maps descriptor.Key against generated constants by string match. Acceptable for a dynamic catalog, but the Type/InputKind control-kind vocabulary in particular is a closed set with no shared enum.

Gender is typed as raw string? rather than a coded closed-set type. Unlike other identifier sets in the codebase, there is no EnumCodec/[Code]-backed Gender enum (confirmed: no `enum Gender` exists), so gender values flow as free-form strings with no stable wire vocab. This is a consistency gap vs the canonical EnumCodec pattern, though gender may intentionally be free-text imported metadata. Low severity — flagging as a design observation, not a hard violation.

CapabilitySubtitles.Item.Format and SourceFormat are bare `string`s for the served subtitle format ('vtt') and original format; there is no SubtitleFormat enum/const, so producers hardcode "vtt" (e.g. Application/Jobs/Handlers/Identity/ExtractSubtitlesJobHandler.cs:51 passes literal "vtt"). Subtitle Source is correctly enum-typed (EntitySubtitleSource) — Format should get the same treatment to close the drift gap.

Public constructor and all public properties (Start/End/Label) have no XML documentation comments, unlike every sibling capability in this cluster and contrary to the repo Quality Bar requiring doc comments on public ctors/members. Minor consistency/documentation gap, not a behavior bug.

AddChild mutates the passed child's ParentEntityId/SortOrder directly (lines 326-327) while a separate HydrateStructuralPlacement path also sets them; combined with the private EntityLink also tracking structural-ness, structural placement is owned by two mechanisms (parent's links vs. child's own fields) that can disagree if a child is added to two parents in memory. Worth noting as an aggregate-boundary correctness smell, not a magic string.

CollectionItemSource (manual/dynamic) and CollectionMode (manual/dynamic/hybrid) share overlapping member codes ('manual','dynamic') across two separate enums. This is intentional and correct (distinct closed sets, distinct columns), but the duplicated literal strings mean a rename of one concept will not be caught by the compiler against the other. No action required; noting only as a low-severity drift-awareness point.

All 15 enums declare `namespace Prismedia.Domain.Entities;` despite living in physically nested folders (Collections/, Core/, Galleries/, Media/, Operations/, Providers/). The folder taxonomy is purely organizational and does not map to namespaces. This is a deliberate flat-namespace convention (consistent across the cluster), so it is not a defect, but the folder/namespace mismatch can surprise contributors expecting folder-aligned namespaces.

EntityStorageShape codes (folder/file/archive/archive-entry) are duplicated as raw string literals in seed data inside Infrastructure migrations (e.g. 20260528134807_InitialPrismediaSchema.cs:1116 `"archive-entry"`, and StorageShape="archive-entry" across multiple Designer/snapshot files). Migrations are immutable historical artifacts so hardcoded literals there are expected, but the live seed source (if any) should reference EnumCodec<EntityStorageShape> rather than re-typing the code. The declaration file itself is correct; flag is informational about downstream consumers outside this cluster.

The metadata attribute references concrete CLR types in Prismedia.Domain.Media and Prismedia.Domain.Taxonomy via the clrType parameter, applied in EntityKind.cs. EntityKind (a foundational closed-set enum) thus carries a compile-time dependency on every concrete aggregate type. This is intentional/centralized (single source of truth) but creates a coupling cycle within the Domain layer: low-level kind taxonomy depends on high-level aggregates. Acceptable as designed but worth noting as a layering tension.

ContainableKinds is a hardcoded allowlist of EntityKind members embedded in the aggregate. It silently omits Gallery-adjacent or future media kinds (e.g. AudioLibrary, VideoSeason, MusicArtist) with no metadata-driven derivation; new media kinds will not be containable unless an author remembers to edit this list. Consider deriving from EntityKindMeta (e.g. an 'isCollectable' facet) rather than a manual set, consistent with the registry-driven approach used elsewhere.

RelationshipKind defines both 'cast' and 'credits' as distinct codes whose doc comments overlap heavily (cast = people credited; credits = credit links projected as credit metadata). The semantic boundary between the two is subtle and undocumented beyond the inline remarks; potential for consumers to pick the wrong one. Not a bug, but a clarity/maintainability concern for a dispatch vocabulary.

VideoSeries.Status is a freeform string? with no shared constant or enum, no validation, and no [Code]-backed closed set. Series status (e.g. airing/ended/cancelled) is a closed-set identifier candidate; as freeform text it invites drift and inconsistent wire/db values across the codebase. Consider an EnumCodec<T>-backed status enum consistent with the canonical [Code] identifier system, or document why it must stay freeform.

Person exposes many freeform string? descriptors (Gender, Ethnicity, EyeColor, HairColor, Measurements, Tattoos, Piercings, Country). Gender/EyeColor/HairColor/Country are realistically closed-ish sets but are modeled as unconstrained text with no normalization, so values can drift between scrapers/UI. Only SetCountry has a mutator; the rest are constructor-only with private setters, which is an inconsistent mutation surface for an aggregate.

Band-member roles are stored as the freeform credit label on CapabilityCredits (per the doc comment, e.g. 'Drummer'/'Composer') rather than via the typed CreditRole [Code] enum. This bypasses the canonical credit-role vocabulary and makes member roles undiscoverable/un-normalizable. Worth confirming whether CreditRole should back these labels.

ConditionAppliesToKind (line 155-160) contains a tautological dead branch: `if (et == "video" && kindCode == "video") return true;` is fully subsumed by the preceding `if (et == kindCode) return true;`. Dead/confusing logic that hints the helper was meant to alias related video kinds (movie/series children) but does not.

MimeForExtension here and MimeForExtension in AudioSourceService.cs are two separate extension->MIME switch tables (image+video here, audio there) both keying off MediaContentTypes constants. The extension-to-mime mapping logic is duplicated across services rather than centralized in one MediaContentTypes resolver, risking divergence for shared extensions like .ogg (video here, audio there).

Five capability mappers (Dates, Positions, Sources, Stats, and to a lesser degree Fingerprints) are near-identical Code/Value(+Label) multi-row hydrate/clear/persist shapes: AsNoTracking + Where(EntityId) + OrderBy(Code) + map to Item; ClearAsync RemoveRange; PersistAsync loop add. This is heavy structural duplication that could be factored into a shared generic base (e.g. CodeValueCapabilityMapper<TRow,TItem>) so a fix/index change is made once.

PersistAsync uses a different capability-access path than its siblings: it calls entity.GetCapability<CapabilityFingerprints>() (line 30) whereas peers read a typed property (entity.Dates, entity.Source, entity.Stats, entity.Position). Inconsistent accessor style across mappers; either expose a typed entity.Fingerprints property or standardize all mappers on GetCapability<T>() so the access pattern is uniform.

Discover<TMapper> (lines 37-45) reflects over the assembly and calls Activator.CreateInstance(type, db) assuming every mapper has exactly a single PrismediaDbContext ctor parameter. There is no guard: a mapper added with a different ctor signature throws at runtime (MissingMethodException) instead of being caught at registration with a clear message. Given this is also used by DI and tests, a clearer failure or a constructor check would harden it.

Persist guards are inconsistent across the single-row mappers: Classification (lines 27-28) and Description (line 27) and Technical (HasData) guard against empty values, Lifetime guards all-null, Progress guards a hand-rolled multi-field OR (line 36), but several rely on the capability simply being absent. The Progress guard in particular is a fragile bespoke condition (Index != 0 || Total != 0 ...) that can drop legitimately zero-valued progress; a consistent IsEmpty()/HasData() convention on the capability would centralize this rule.

ChipIcon is a parallel kind-to-presentation table that must be kept in sync with every new EntityKind added. EntityKindMeta already centralizes per-kind facts (category, storage shape, group label, clr type) via reflection; the icon glyph is a natural additional field there. As written, adding a new kind silently maps to the generic 'count' icon until someone remembers to extend this switch — a maintenance/drift hazard rather than a hard error.

MovieKindMapper injects PrismediaDbContext purely to ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull it in the constructor and never uses it; every other kind mapper uses the primary-constructor `(PrismediaDbContext db)` form. This is dead/inconsistent dependency injection — Movie has no detail table, so the db parameter could be dropped entirely (or kept for symmetry, but then it should match the primary-constructor style used by all siblings).

ProjectDetail unconditionally builds VideoDetail and re-casts via `(entity as Video)?.SubtitleCapability` at line 46 instead of the pattern-matched `is Video video` guard the other mappers use; if a non-Video entity were ever routed here it would produce a VideoDetail with a null subtitle field rather than the bare card. VideoSeriesKindMapper has the same unconditional-projection shape. Minor inconsistency vs. the `entity is X ? new XDetail{...} : card` pattern used by Book/Collection/Gallery/Person/Tag/AudioTrack.

Each kind mapper repeats a private `Track(...DetailRow row)` helper that just does `db.Xxx.Add(row); return row;` (AudioTrack, BookChapter, Book, Collection, Gallery, Person, Tag, Video, VideoSeries — 9 copies). This is benign boilerplate but is a clear duplication candidate; a small generic helper on a base class or extension (e.g. `db.AddAndReturn(row)`) would remove ~9 near-identical methods. Not a correctness issue.

GetDetailAsync recursively enumerates the whole directory subtree (EnumerateFiles AllDirectories) to compute file count and total size synchronously inside a Task.FromResult method. For large folders this blocks and can be slow; the directory-size computation has no depth/time bound.

Previewability (IsPreviewable) re-parses MIME strings with StartsWith('image/','video/',...) and re-lists 'application/json'/'application/xml' literally, duplicating the MIME knowledge already encoded in DetectMimeType and MediaContentTypes; a single MIME catalog would remove this duplicated classification logic.

EPUB cover temp file is written with a generic '.img' extension (raw cover bytes of unknown type), while PDF uses '.jpg'. Downstream thumbnailing must sniff the format; an extensionless/typed temp path would be more robust. Minor correctness/robustness note.

Both ReadPdf and ReadEpubAsync swallow all exceptions and return null, losing the failure cause (no logging). Combined with the catch-all in BookCoverImageExtractor, book-import failures are silent; consider logging before returning null.

Each Upsert* method calls _db.SaveChangesAsync individually; during a scan the worker invokes many of these per entity (technical, source+streams, file, fingerprint, subtitle, tags) producing N round-trips per item with no batching/transaction boundary. The batched UpsertVideosBatchAsync exists for entities but the downstream processing writes remain one-save-per-call. Performance/consistency note, not a correctness bug.

hasUsableSubtitleState calls File.Exists per subtitle row synchronously inside the batch-needs computation (infrastructure persistence service doing filesystem IO). For large batches this is synchronous disk IO on the request/job thread; consider hoisting filesystem checks out of the persistence-read path or batching them.

RunFfprobeAsync swallows all non-zero exit codes and JsonException/InvalidOperationException by returning null with no logging. A probe failure is indistinguishable from 'file has no streams', which can silently mis-classify media during scan. Consider surfacing/logging the ffprobe stderr at least at debug level.

ParseBitDepth infers bit depth by substring-scanning the pixel format for '12'/'10'/'9'/'8' in order. This is fragile: a format like 'yuv420p10le' works, but any pix_fmt containing an unrelated digit (or future formats) could mis-read. Acceptable heuristic but worth a comment/whitelist of known pix_fmts.

EntityProgressRow carries several open-vocabulary string columns (Unit default 'item', Mode, Location) with no enum/code backing, unlike the rest of the codebase's [Code]-enum discipline. Combined with the bare 'item' default also hardcoded in domain (CapabilityProgress) and application (EntityCapabilityService) layers, the progress-unit concept has three independent default literals that can drift.

Video and Image extension sets deliberately overlap (.webm/.mp4/.mkv/.mov/.avi/.wmv/.flv appear in both) so a file can match two categories. This is intentional (gallery clips) but there is no documented precedence rule here for how discovery resolves an ambiguous extension; the tie-break lives elsewhere (file discovery), making this set hard to reason about in isolation.

This row is a denormalized persisted projection of the canonical EntityKind + [EntityKindMeta]/[Code] registry (Code/DisplayName/Category/StorageShape). It duplicates data that is the source of truth in the domain registry; the seeded migration data (InitialPrismediaSchema.cs:1111+) must be hand-kept in sync with the EntityKindRegistry. Reasonable as a read cache, but it is a second source of kind metadata that can drift from the [EntityKindMeta] attributes (Category/StorageShape are typed enums in the domain but flat strings here).

MediaStreamRow.Type (line 28) is a free-form string ('video'/'audio'/'subtitle' style stream-kind) with no typed enum or canonical code backing it, unlike most other closed-set identifiers in this codebase which use [Code] enums. Stream type is a closed set and a strong candidate for a typed MediaStreamType enum/[Code] to match the EnumCodec gold standard.

relationship_code and target_kind_code are persisted as plain HasMaxLength(64) strings with no EnumCodec HasConversion, unlike most other code columns in sibling configs (which use ToCode()/DecodeAs<T>()). These are closed-set codes (RelationshipKind / EntityKind) per the project's canonical codec system, so the persistence boundary is weaker here than the gold-standard pattern used in CollectionDetailRow/JobRunRow/etc. Inconsistent codec adoption.

ix_job_runs_dedup is named explicitly but the dedup semantics it backs (Type+TargetEntityId+Status uniqueness/lookup) live in handler code; fine as-is, but note TargetEntityId is stored as a HasMaxLength(64) string (line 255) rather than a Guid, so job target identity is a stringly-typed key across the job system. Worth flagging as a typing weakness, not a bug.

ApplyStructuralSortOrderAsync is declared async but contains no await (the only async work, ApplyStructuralSortOrderAsync's body, is synchronous after EntityMetadataPositionRules.SortOrderFor). It takes a CancellationToken it never uses and returns a completed Task — should be synchronous or actually honor cancellation.

FindEntityInProposalTree only walks proposal.Children, not proposal.Relationships, and CascadeFromParentAsync relies on it to locate the target entity in the parent's resolved tree. If the target ever surfaces as a relationship node this returns null and the cascade silently yields no match; acceptable given relationships are person/studio/tag, but the asymmetry is undocumented.

Hardcoded spoofed Chrome User-Agent embedded in the engine. Not a magic identifier, but it is a fixed external-facing constant that will age and is untestable/unconfigurable; consider sourcing from configuration.

ToContract constructs LibraryRoot positionally with 14 args where ScanBooks/IsNsfw/AutoIdentify ordering must exactly match the contract; positional record construction across a 14-field projection is fragile to reordering. Low risk but worth named-argument construction or a mapping helper.

The Jellyfin quality ladder, CRF-by-height table, H264 codec-string-by-height table, and bitrate heuristics (the 1.5x efficient-codec inflation, 20Mbps cap) are hardcoded transcode-policy data embedded in infrastructure. This is application/domain playback policy living in the infrastructure layer; per the backend architecture contract such decision tables would be better positioned as a policy type the infrastructure consumes.

Minor inconsistency: BuildImagesOnlyPlaylist uses info.TileWidth/TileHeight as the tile column/row LAYOUT, but the fallback TrickplayInfoRow (GetInfoAsync:148) populates TileWidth=DefaultTileColumns(5) and TileHeight=DefaultTileRows(5) — i.e. column/row counts are stored in fields named Width/Height. The naming conflates tile pixel dimensions with grid layout counts, which is error-prone for future edits.

The hook correctly references the shared PAGE_CACHE_CONTROL constant (no duplication on the production side) and is otherwise clean. Minor: isPageDocumentResponse hardcodes the framework's /__data.json suffix and /api/ prefix as bare literals; fine in isolation, but they are the kind of routing scalar that could drift if reused elsewhere — consider a small named const if a second consumer appears.

Whitebox CSS-string test: it reads src/app.css and matches selectors/regex against its raw text. Correct given there is no other way to assert global CSS, but it is brittle to formatting changes (e.g. the regex `\n {2}\}` assumes a 2-space-indented closing brace for the coarse-pointer media block). Not a magic-string issue, just a fragility note.

isRecord type guard is duplicated: list-prefs.ts exports it (consumed by filter-presets.ts and entity-grid-prefs.ts) but nav/nav-catalog.ts:94 defines its own private copy of the same guard. Consolidate onto the exported one.

Three parallel asc/desc sort-direction declarations across lib: SortDir (list-prefs.ts:19), FilterPreset.sortDir (filter-presets.ts:24), and EntityGridSortDir (entities/entity-grid.ts:35, plus VALID_SORT_DIRS array in entity-grid-prefs.ts:80).
