They should share one SortDir source of truth to avoid divergence.

buildStudioEditPatch/buildTagEditPatch return inferred anonymous object shapes (no declared return type) that are intended to be update-API request bodies, but they are not tied to a generated request DTO. If the API contract for studio/tag update changes, these silently drift and only fail at the call site. Worth annotating with the generated patch type. (No current Svelte consumer found via grep — only the test imports them, so they may be dead or newly added.)

ui-prefs read/write fetch logic (path + PUT body shape `{ value }`) is reimplemented independently in prefs/ui-list-prefs-writer.ts:18; both hand-build the `/ui-prefs/{key}` URL and PUT payload. A single ui-prefs client would prevent the path/body contract from drifting between the two writers.

ReleaseUpdateStatus is a hand-written DTO duplicating the backend update-check response shape rather than a generated client type. version.test.ts fixtures don't include the `channel` field that the interface declares (line 12) and version.ts never reads `error`, indicating the FE/BE contract is maintained by hand and already partially out of sync.

ServerPresetsApi re-exports the same FilterPreset type and surface as the localStorage createFilterPresets but is an entirely separate implementation. The two preset stores (local + server) can drift in behavior; consider one interface with a pluggable backing store rather than two parallel APIs.

keepFlyoutOnScreen unconditionally overwrites node.style.transform on every applyLayout pass (line 72 clears it, line 77 sets translateX or empty). Any component-supplied transform on the flyout node (e.g. an entrance/scale animation) is silently clobbered, so this action cannot compose with transform-based transitions. Not a correctness bug for the current pure-shift use, but a layering constraint worth noting.

The action's update() defaults nextOptions to {} when called with no args, so a Svelte `use:keepFlyoutOnScreen={opts}` whose opts becomes undefined would reset to default gutter rather than preserving currentOptions. Minor — current call sites always pass an options object — but the default differs from typical 'merge/keep previous' update semantics.

portal action does not re-parent when the `target` parameter changes after mount (no update() handler returned). If a consumer reactively swaps the portal target, the node stays in the original parent. Acceptable for the current fixed-body usage but an implicit limitation.

closeBulkIdentifySession(_sessionId) is a no-op stub returning Promise.resolve() and ignores its argument; the bulk-identify session lifecycle it implies is not actually wired up, which is a latent correctness gap if callers assume server-side teardown happens.

fetchOptionalIdentifyQueueItem hand-rolls fetch + 404 handling instead of using the generated getIdentifyQueueItem client (used by fetchIdentifyQueueItem one function above), duplicating the request-building/error-throwing logic that unwrapGenerated already centralizes.

Pervasive 'as Generated*' casts between local $lib/collections/models types and generated request types (createCollection, updateCollection, addCollectionItems, etc.). The casts silence the compiler at the facade boundary, so a mismatch between the local model and the generated contract would not be caught.

fetchApi (line 38) and orvalFetch (line 81) duplicate the same fetch+headers+content-type+error-text logic; they differ only in return shape (raw JSON vs {data,status,headers}). The shared core (header building, credentials default, !ok error throw, text->JSON parse) should be factored into one helper to avoid the two paths drifting.

Repeated `as unknown as Parameters<typeof fn>[n]` casts to force loosely-typed payloads into the generated request signatures defeat the generated-contract type safety at the API boundary; indicates the local SettingValue/Partial<LibraryRoot> request shapes are not aligned with the generated request models.

COLLECTION_RULE_FIELDS rows repeat the operator string literals inline for every field (e.g. 'contains','greater_than', etc.) rather than referencing operator groups by field type, so the same operator sets are copy-pasted across many rows and must be kept consistent with the CollectionOperator union by hand.

Waveform load has a two-path fallback (track.waveformPath OR a hardcoded /assets/audio-tracks/<id>/waveform.json convention) and parses an untyped {data?: number[]} JSON shape inline. The asset-path convention is duplicated knowledge that belongs in a shared asset helper/contract; the ad-hoc shape parsing has no shared type.

SEGMENT_LABELS hardcodes a slug->label map (movies/videos/people) separate from the route/entity tables in entity-codes; breadcrumb labels for route segments can drift from the actual route definitions and from ENTITY_KIND_LABELS. resolveHref() is also an identity no-op left in place (dead indirection).

These tests assert on raw component source text (readFile + toContain on class names / variable names / markup) rather than rendered behavior. They are brittle implementation-detail tests that break on harmless refactors and don't verify actual rendering; prefer DOM/behavioral assertions like ComicReader.test.ts does.

Search debounce/race handling is duplicated: an $effect (lines 160-188) sets loading and schedules runSearch, and runSearch ALSO re-validates length<2 and re-increments activeRequest, so the request-id guard logic lives in two places and the loading flag is set twice. Centralizing into a single debounced search helper would remove the duplicated guard/race logic.

Inline client-side filtering re-implements the search/filter pattern (lowercase substring match on title) that SearchSelect/forms primitives already provide; multiple pickers (DestinationPicker, LibraryRootPicker, CommandPalette) each hand-roll their own filter input + matching logic.

Release-channel display logic (docsHref, channelLabel hiding when channel==='release', update LED) is duplicated from the desktop sidebar footer (comment at line 49-50 says it mirrors the desktop sidebar). The 'release' channel literal and the version/channel badge rendering should live in one shared component or helper so the two footers cannot drift.

jumpTo(playback.position + 1 + i) reaches into the audio store's internal index arithmetic from the view. The 'jump to the Nth up-next track' intent would be safer as a dedicated store method (e.g. jumpToUpNext(i)) so position/order bookkeeping stays encapsulated in audio-playback.svelte.ts.

Time parse/format (parseTimeInput/formatSecondsInput) plus the commit-on-blur/readCommittedTimes orchestration is non-trivial and player-adjacent; TrackListRow has its own formatDuration too. Centralizing seconds<->mm:ss formatting in a shared util would remove duplicate time-formatting logic across player/marker/track components.

Test asserts on the raw component source string (exact grid-template-areas text incl. embedded newline) rather than rendered DOM/computed style; brittle to whitespace/formatting changes that do not alter behavior.

A hidden (class="hidden") delete <button> is rendered when onDelete is set, duplicating the flyout delete action; appears to be dead/leftover markup that adds an unused interactive element to the a11y tree.

Two separate harness components (.test-harness and .details-test-harness) wrap UniversalLightbox with near-identical provideNsfw setup; the details harness duplicates the base harness plus a detailsContent snippet and could be a single parameterized harness.

Every Lucide import is double-cast `as unknown as Component<Record<string, unknown>>`, defeating type safety for the icon map. A single typed helper or a properly typed icon-component alias would remove ~24 unsafe casts and catch a wrong/missing icon at build time.

waveformForDisplay computes pairCount = floor(length/2) but only uses it for a >0 check, which is equivalent to length>=2; a single-sample waveform (length 1) returns null. Either the pair semantics are unused/misleading or single-sample payloads are intentionally dropped — the intent is unclear and untested for the length===1 case.

Animated-still detection (animatedImageFormats = {gif, apng}) and mime/extension parsing live inline in the view component; this image-format domain knowledge overlaps with universal-lightbox-media helpers and would be better centralized so feed and lightbox stay consistent on what counts as animated.

DateField generates its id from Math.random() per render (line 32) rather than Svelte's $props.id()/crypto.randomUUID or a stable derived id; harmless but non-deterministic and inconsistent if other form fields use a stable id strategy.

DOM id generation via Math.random().toString(36) is repeated in EntityPicker, SearchSelect, TagSelect, MarkdownEditor, TextField, TextAreaField (and DateField). A shared useId/$props.id() helper would centralize this; minor but pervasive duplication.

Accesses editor.storage as Record<string,any> and casts to any to reach the tiptap-markdown getMarkdown() API, bypassing types; acceptable as a localized adapter but worth a typed wrapper if the markdown storage shape is used elsewhere.

id uses Math.random() for the listbox aria id (`provider-select-${Math.random()...}`), generated at module/component init. Prefer Svelte's $props.id()/useId-style stable id; random ids are non-deterministic and can hurt SSR hydration and test stability.

visibleProviders caps results at 50 via slice(0,50) with no overflow indication; a household with many providers would silently lose entries from the search list. Minor but worth a 'showing N of M' affordance or removing the cap.

Duplicate bulk-queue wiring: the toolbar 'Queue N' button (handleBulkQueue) and the EntityGrid bulkActions onRun both call store.startBulk with filteredEntities filtered by ids — two parallel entry points for the same action that can diverge. Consolidate to one handler.

Manual reactive guard (loadedEntityKind / loadRequestId) reimplements request-cancellation/dedup inside an $effect. Works, but this race-handling boilerplate is exactly the kind of thing a shared async-resource helper should own; flagged as a layering/duplication smell across identify load sites.

VISUAL_KINDS is a hand-maintained Set of ENTITY_KIND values (book/video/movie/image/gallery/videoSeries/videoSeason). Good that it references ENTITY_KIND constants, but it omits movie? no — includes movie; however it silently excludes audio/music/collection kinds with no central definition of 'visual kind'. Consider a shared isVisualKind helper so the list does not drift from the kind registry.

IdentifyStore is a very large (1300+ line) single class mixing queue state, provider-search orchestration, two independent polling loops (cascade + apply-progress), bulk flows, and per-proposal review selection maps. It is well-documented but a candidate for splitting (e.g. a ReviewSelectionState slice and a polling/orchestration slice) to reduce the surface that must change together.

countStructuralApplyProgressSteps / countApplyProgressSteps assume proposal.patch.credits/tags are always arrays and studio is a string, while other code paths guard patch fields with ?? — inconsistent null-handling of the patch shape risks a runtime throw if a proposal arrives with absent arrays.

buildLightboxVideoSources assumes the generated preview is always video/mp4 (hardcoded 'fallback','video/mp4') regardless of the original format; correct for the current pipeline but couples this helper to that backend assumption with no shared constant.

This regression test asserts behavior by string-grepping component source (readFile + expect(src).toContain("showControls") / 'if (flow !== "paged") {'). It is brittle: any benign refactor/rename of those identifiers breaks the test without a behavior change, and it does not actually verify runtime toolbar behavior. Acknowledged in-file as a jsdom workaround, but worth flagging as fragile coupling to source text.

NumberStepper, QualitySlider, and the inline integer/decimal controls in SettingsControl each re-derive an input id, hold a local draft, and clamp on commit independently. This stepper is also defined but appears redundant with SettingsControl's integer variant; verify it is still used, otherwise it is dead duplication.

Defines formatBytes locally (lines 88-99) and BYTES_PER_GB; byte formatting is a common util likely duplicated elsewhere in the app and should live in a shared formatting module rather than inside a settings section component.

Three parallel local maps (videoExtensions, videoContainers, videoMimeTypes) encode container/extension/codec->video knowledge entirely client-side. This duplicates format/container vocabulary that the backend technical-capability layer also knows; drift risk and no shared source of truth for which containers/codecs are 'video'. Consider deriving from a shared contract or at least colocating with media-core helpers.

Very large single component (~1440 lines, ~500 of script) owning gesture state machines (touch direction disambiguation, pointer capture/scrub, hover-intent timer), sprite/sequence asset resolution, selection, and styling. The gesture/scrub logic is self-contained and testable and could be extracted into a hook/module (entity-thumbnail-scrub.ts) to shrink the component and isolate the pointer/touch correctness logic.

mimeTypeForEntity resolves mime by checking technical.format then technical.container then title extension against videoMimeTypes; the 'h264'/'hevc'/'h265'/'vp8'/'vp9'/'av1' keys are codecs mixed into the same map as containers/extensions, so a codec value landing in format/container fields maps opportunistically. Works, but conflates codec and container namespaces in one lookup which can mislabel mime (e.g. hevc->video/quicktime).

Lab fixtures fabricate EntityCapability objects with raw kind strings and use provider "stashdb" which is not in EXTERNAL_ID_PROVIDER (canonical is "stash"); several builders (tags/studio/credits) are stubs that all return a flags capability (lines 39-49), so the fixture does not actually exercise those capability shapes despite appearing to. Fixture drift risk if the canonical capability/provider codes change.

Three functions (searchTags/searchPeople/searchStudios) are byte-for-byte identical except the imported list endpoint; collapsing into a single factory parameterized by the list fn would remove the copy-paste and the duplicated {query,limit:20} param shaping.

formatDateCode (line 192) and formatStatCode (line 196) are byte-identical (both replaceAll('-',' ') + title-case) and also duplicate the same transform in entity-codes.labelForEntityKind fallback and entity-grid.statLabel. A single humanizeCode() utility should own this kebab-to-title-case formatting.

The hover-kind discriminator vocabulary (none/trickplay/image-sequence/sprite) and the aspect-ratio names (square/video/poster/portrait/wide) are typed unions only; consumers in entity-grid.ts, thumbnail-lab-data.ts, and lab-data-helpers.ts repeat the bare string literals (e.g. hover.kind === 'sprite', kind: 'trickplay'). Not magic identifiers in the dispatch-key sense, but extracting const objects (HOVER_KIND, ASPECT_RATIO) would let fixtures/builders reference them instead of re-spelling.
Only MediaSources[0] is ever read; multi-source playback responses are silently ignored. Acceptable for current single-source flow but worth noting as an implicit assumption with no guard or comment.

Every test fixture hardcodes capability kind literals and Jellyfin stream-type literals that mirror production constants (CAPABILITY_KIND.*, ENTITY_FILE_ROLE.*, JellyfinProtocol stream types). Because EntityCapability.kind is typed loosely in fixtures, a rename of a generated code would not break these tests, weakening their value as a regression guard for the code system.

fileTreeRootPath duplicates the root display-name fallback logic verbatim inside its own duplicate-detection loop: `root.label?.trim() || root.path?.split(/[\\/]/).filter(Boolean).at(-1) || "Library"` appears at lines 13 and 16. The inner computation should reuse a single local helper to avoid drift between the label and the duplicate-comparison label.

Two different naming conventions for the synthetic root node: createFileTreeRegistry sets name = `root.label || root.path` (falsy-empty-string fallback), whereas fileTreeRootPath uses `root.label?.trim() || ...path-last-segment... || "Library"`. The registry node `name` can therefore diverge from the displayed tree path label for whitespace-only or empty labels. Minor correctness/consistency issue in how the root is labeled.

Tree-path string scheme (slash-joined display path, directories suffixed with trailing '/') is an ad hoc serialization used as a Map key and for ancestor/descendant tests (isSameOrDescendantTreePath via startsWith). It is purely string-prefix based, so a directory whose display name is a prefix of a sibling could in principle collide; correctness leans on the trailing-slash guard. Acceptable but worth noting as fragile string-keying rather than structured node ids.

elementInView and elementSize (element-size.svelte.ts) duplicate the same hand-rolled rune+observer+`attach` use-action+destroy-teardown boilerplate. Reasonable as-is, but the observer lifecycle pattern could be factored into a single shared `createObserverHook` helper to avoid drift between the two (and any future observer hooks).

The doc comment promises this value is for the `from` query parameter, but that contract (the literal `from` key) lives in callers, not here. Not a magic string in this file; flagged only as a cross-file coupling note — the `from` key consumers should reference a shared constant rather than each hardcoding 'from'.

NSFW redaction logic keys on queueName === 'library-maintenance' and rewrites headings/triggers/details to generic 'file layout' strings. This couples a privacy/redaction concern to a magic queue-name literal in a presentation helper; if the maintenance queue is renamed the redaction silently stops applying. Worth centralizing the 'redactable queue' decision behind a named predicate/constant.

error is set to job.message only when status==='failed', and updatedAt is synthesized as finishedAt ?? startedAt ?? createdAt because the API DTO has no updatedAt/error fields. Minor: the JobRun.attempts field is always 0 (line 235) since the DTO has no attempts; consumers that surface attempts will always show 0.
The string "Escape" is written twice — once in the isTyping guard (line 19) and once in the switch (line 22). Minor duplication/drift risk; a single local key constant would keep them in sync.

extraKeys lookup is keyed by raw event.key, so callers must hardcode the same DOM key strings to register handlers; there is no shared key vocabulary, meaning each call site independently hardcodes keys like 'f' or ' '. Acceptable for a small helper but a candidate for a shared KEY constant set if more keyboard handlers adopt this pattern.

DEFAULT_MOBILE_FAVORITES is a free-standing literal list of route hrefs that is NOT validated against the catalog. If any of these slugs is removed/renamed in app-shell-sections.ts, resolveFavorites silently skips it (take() no-ops on a missing href) with no test or type error, leaving a smaller-than-intended default bar. Consider asserting at build/test time that every DEFAULT_MOBILE_FAVORITES entry exists in buildNavCatalog().

normalizeNavPrefs reads parsed.v and parsed.version but the persisted/cookie write path (NavPrefs uses `v: 1`) and the server document (uses `version`) are two different key conventions reconciled only here. This dual-key handling is undocumented contract drift between the cookie shape and the server layout document; worth a single typed boundary/comment so the two producers don't diverge further.

mobileFavorites is capped to MAX_MOBILE_FAVORITES in normalizeNavPrefs via slice, but the persisted NavPrefs interface (line 33-34) documents the max-4 invariant only in a comment and resolveFavorites re-applies the cap (line 214). The cap is enforced in three places (interface comment, normalize, resolve); a single guarded constructor/helper would prevent any path producing an over-length list.

Untyped response shape: the /api/client-info call casts the JSON to an inline `{ isLan?: boolean }` ad-hoc type rather than a generated contract type, bypassing the OpenAPI-generated client the repo standardizes on. Drifts from the 'prefer generated typed contracts' rule.

Test-without-source in this cluster: timeline-chapters.test.ts imports ./timeline-chapters (buildTimelineChapterCues, findTimelineChapterTitle) but the implementation file timeline-chapters.ts was not part of the assigned cluster, so I could not review the implementation it covers.

Codec-id knowledge is duplicated across the player module: EFFICIENT_CODECS (quality-ladder) and the videoCodecBadge alias groups (media-badges) each maintain their own lowercase codec-id lists with no shared codec vocabulary, so a new codec alias must be added in multiple places.

Module-level mutable cueCache (Map keyed by `${track.id}:${track.url}`) is a process-lifetime singleton that is never invalidated/evicted; a track whose underlying VTT is regenerated (e.g. re-extracted subtitles) keeps serving the stale cached promise, and the map grows unbounded across navigation. Consider bounding/invalidating it or keying on a content version.

parseWebVttCues is a hand-rolled WebVTT parser (block split, NOTE/STYLE/WEBVTT skipping, timestamp regex, tag stripping) that duplicates parsing concerns the player's vidstack/VTTCue stack already understands; the trickplay path uses loadTrickplayFrames from @prismedia/ui-svelte for its VTT. Two independent VTT parsers in the same player area risk divergent handling (e.g. cue settings, escapes). Consider consolidating VTT parsing into one shared utility.

parseWebVttTimestamp returns 0 for any timestamp that is not 2 or 3 colon-parts and does no NaN guarding on Number(...) conversions, so a malformed cue silently yields start/end of 0 rather than being rejected; combined with the caller pushing the cue anyway this can emit a 0-duration cue at the timeline origin.

Stale doc comment: the JSDoc says 'Translate the cookie-backed list prefs' but the module header (lines 1-12) states these prefs are persisted to the ui_prefs DB table (not cookies) via createServerPrefs. The same 'cookie-backed' framing appears in the comment block; misleading after the cookie->ui_prefs migration.

writeListPrefs swallows all errors silently (empty catch) by design (best-effort), but writeListPrefsAndInvalidate awaits it and then unconditionally invalidates even when the PUT failed; the comment promises the server 'will see the new value on re-run' which is not guaranteed on a failed write. Acceptable as documented best-effort, but the invalidate-after-failed-write can show the user stale state with no signal.

detectUiPrefsFormFactor has a side effect (rememberUiPrefsFormFactor writes a cookie) inside what reads like a pure detector; the test (form-factor-prefs.test.ts:27-38) stubs matchMedia but does not assert/guard the cookie write, and there is no window/document guard around writeCookie when called from the matchMedia branch (it is only reached client-side, so currently safe). Consider splitting detection from the cookie-persistence side effect for testability and SSR clarity.

score (2/1) and subtitle composition are computed inline inside entityToSearchItem with the relevance weights as magic numbers tied to matchType. The ranking weights are not named or centralized, so direct-vs-related ordering semantics are implicit; extracting the weights (and the 'Related to' subtitle template) would make the ranking contract explicit and testable.

hlsTranscoderProfiles is a closed enum maintained only on the frontend with no link to the backend transcoder-profile codes; if the backend HLS profile names change, this picker silently desyncs. Consider sourcing from generated codes or a documented shared list.

restore() mutates this.pendingSurfaces while iterating over this.surfaces and deleting from pendingSurfaces inside the loop. The delete is on a different map than the one being iterated so it is technically safe, but the dual-map handoff (pendingSurfaces seeded then drained by both restore() and registerSurface()) is subtle: if restore() is called twice in a row, the second call overwrites pendingSurfaces, silently dropping any not-yet-registered surfaces from the first restore. Acceptable for history-restore use but worth a guard/comment.

toggleShuffle assumes this.position is a valid index into this.order when order.length>0 (uses this.order[this.position]!). If position is -1 (queue loaded but cleared to -1) but order non-empty, this.order[-1] is undefined and the non-null assertion masks it. In practice play()/clear() keep these consistent, but the invariant is implicit rather than guarded.

Module-level singleton (new DismissedErrorsStore()) with manual init() that reads localStorage, diverging from the rest of the cluster which uses createContext/createOptionalContext provider pattern (app-chrome, audio-playback, nav-customization, page-snapshots, search). recent-searches/selection use a rune-factory pattern. Three different store idioms coexist in one folder; the localStorage-singleton with eager-but-deferred init is the odd one out and is not SSR-guarded by `browser` the way recent-searches.svelte.ts is (it relies on init() never running on the server).

commit() persists every mutation to the server via debounced saveNavLayout, but the NavPrefs interface doc-comment in nav-catalog.ts:24 still says prefs are 'Stored as the prismedia-nav cookie', while this store has moved persistence to the server (fetchNavLayout/saveNavLayout). Stale documentation describing a cookie that is no longer the storage mechanism.

Uses a private `attached` boolean plus $effect.root to guard a single global keydown listener, but $effect.root is never explicitly disposed by the store (no stored dispose handle), so the listener lifetime is tied to the root effect rather than the store instance. For a context-provided singleton this is fine, but if provideSearch() is ever called more than once the guard only prevents double-binding within one instance, not across instances.

Library-root resolution precedence (explicit.rootId ?? target.libraryRootId ?? this.resolvedRootId) is repeated verbatim in three uploadOne branches, and the audio variant repeats the same pattern for audioLibraryId. Same fallback chain duplicated; extract a resolveRoot()/resolveAudioLibrary() helper so the 'No library root selected' guard isn't re-implemented per kind.

runUploads rebuilds the entire this.files array with .map() on every per-file status transition (pending->uploading->done/error) inside the loop — O(n^2) array reallocation for large batches. Functional but a correctness/perf smell for big drops; an indexed in-place update (with $state reassignment) would be cheaper.

uploadTargetLabel returns `${categoryForTarget(target)} files` using the raw lowercase kind code as user-facing display text (e.g. 'video files'). ENTITY_KIND_LABELS exists in codes.ts for exactly this kind->label mapping; the label is derived from the dispatch code instead of the canonical display-label map, coupling wire code to UI copy.

formatResolutionLabel maps video height to resolution labels (4K/1440p/1080p/720p/480p) entirely client-side with hardcoded thresholds. These are display-only here, but the resolution/quality ladder is a domain concept that the player quality picker also computes client-side; per project memory, client tier names must stay aligned with server renditions. Risk of two independent height->label ladders drifting if more callers appear. Worth confirming there is a single shared source for resolution tiers rather than duplicating thresholds across player and formatters.

durationToSeconds and formatDurationString both naively split on ':' and assume an HH:MM:SS shape; durationToSeconds does not strip fractional seconds before Number() (works because Number('12.345') is valid) but neither validates segment count, so a malformed value silently yields a wrong number rather than null. Minor correctness/robustness gap, not a magic-string issue.

Magic limit 20 is duplicated: ACTIVITY_LIMIT=20 is defined and used for continue/recent, but the per-section fetch and the post-fetch slice hardcode literal 20 instead of a named SECTION_LIMIT constant, so the two limits can silently drift.

sections are rebuilt by index-correlating Promise.allSettled results back to SECTION_DEFS[i]; relies on array-position coupling between the mapped promises and the def list. Brittle if the mapping is ever reordered or filtered; carrying def in the resolved value (as the fulfilled branch already does for items) and mapping over that would be safer.

defaultLayoutData is computed at component-init from cookies and then spread under server `data`, while provideNsfw re-reads cookies live; the precedence (cookie vs layoutData.initialNsfwMode/hasNsfwModeCookie) is subtle and split across lines 36-37 and 48-50, making the 'has explicit mode' resolution hard to reason about. Consolidating cookie-vs-data precedence into one helper would reduce correctness ambiguity.

Polling uses setInterval(5000) for both dashboard and worker health with no in-flight guard; if a fetch takes >5s requests overlap. Also two separate $effect blocks plus onMount all trigger loadDashboard, so initial load can fire multiple concurrent fetches (data.dashboard effect, nsfw effect, onMount).

collectLibraryTracks recurses through nested audio sub-libraries with unbounded Promise.all fan-out and no depth/visited guard; a deep or cyclic library graph could trigger a request storm. Play All eagerly fetches every album+sub-library before playing.

All design tokens (core/accent/signal palettes, radii, durations, easing) are re-hardcoded as JS literal arrays of hex/px values in this showcase, duplicating the real CSS custom properties / Tailwind theme. The reference doc can drift from the actual tokens since nothing derives these from the source of truth.

PositionTicks uses the magic factor 10_000_000 (seconds->ticks) inline in three places (L492, L507, L526) and the progress-report interval 10_000ms / 3s threshold are bare literals; these Jellyfin tick conversions should be a shared helper to avoid divergence across player pages.

Test asserts behavior by string-matching the raw .svelte source (toContain("Play All"), toContain("useAudioPlayback"), not.toContain("<AudioVidStackPlayer"), index-of ordering of "Sub-Libraries" vs "<AudioTrackList"). This is brittle: it breaks on cosmetic renames/reorders and passes even if the wired behavior is broken. It also pins the exact UI label "Play All"/"Shuffle", coupling the test to display text. A component/render test (Testing Library) would assert actual behavior instead of source text.

Parent-cover fetch is a fire-and-forget promise (.then/.catch(() => {})) outside the awaited Promise.all on the tracks page (tracks/[id] line 123-130), and a similar pattern is used for the album. Errors are swallowed silently and there is no cancellation if the component unmounts / params change before resolution, which can set parentCoverUrl on a stale view. Minor correctness/race smell.

Unused derived: `bookTitle` (line 91 in [id], line 46 in chapters, line 47 in volumes) is declared in the chapter/volume pages but heroMeta uses it while in the book [id] page bookTitle is computed and never referenced. Minor, but indicates copy-paste of the scaffold without pruning.

Loading/error UX diverges from the sibling edit route: detail uses EntityDetailSkeleton + Tailwind error card, edit ([id]/edit/+page.svelte) uses a scoped-CSS pulse loading-shell and error-notice. Two routes in the same feature present different load/error scaffolding. Consolidate on one shared detail loading+error component.

Native confirm() is used for destructive delete (handleDeleteCollection, line 256). Blocking browser confirm bypasses the app's design system / glass modal patterns and is inconsistent with a 'Prism Noir Luxe' UI; should use a shared confirm-dialog primitive.

Two separate error channels coexist (errorMessage for whole-page load failures + itemMutationError for refresh/remove/delete failures) rendered as two different inline banners. The delete handler (line 262) writes failures into itemMutationError, which renders inside the {#if card && collection} block — fine while loaded, but the dual-error model plus duplicated banner markup increases maintenance surface; a single notification mechanism would simplify.

Local naming shadowing: the openImageLightbox parameter is named `card` (line 171) which shadows the top-level `const card` $derived (line 68, the EntityDetailCardFull passed to EntityDetail). Harmless today but easy to confuse during edits.

CSS hardcodes raw hex color fallbacks inline (#ef4444, #101420, #8a93a6, #1c2235, #151a28, #0c0f15, #f2eed8, #c49a5a) across .error-notice/.content-*/.empty-children rather than relying solely on design-token variables. These duplicate token values and risk drift from the Prism Noir Luxe palette; the var(--color-*) fallbacks should come from the token layer, not be re-spelled per route.

lightboxEntity duplicates the entity->lightbox projection logic (id/kind/title/capabilities/coverUrl/isNsfw/rating mapping, lines 48-60) that EntityIndexPage performs via hydrateLightboxEntity/ImageLightboxDetails for the grid lightbox. The detail route and index route build UniversalLightboxEntity differently; this should funnel through one shared projector (e.g. hydrateLightboxEntity / a toUniversalLightboxEntity helper) to avoid drift.

Runtime label "dotnet-process" is hardcoded in both PrismediaCommunityTab (162) and InstalledPluginsTab (404) rather than reading plugin.runtime (which InstalledPlugin already carries). Two hardcoded copies of a runtime identifier that can drift from the real runtime value.

testResult is a single shared object for all endpoints; the per-card guard `result.id === item.id` means only one endpoint can show a connection result at a time, and {@const result = testResult} inside the loop is redundant. Minor correctness/clarity smell if multiple endpoints are tested in sequence.

Breadcrumb hrefs are built as raw template strings ('/series', `/series/${seriesId}`) instead of using resolve()/resolveEntityHref like the series detail page does (which imports resolve from $app/paths). Inconsistent link construction within the same cluster; route paths are hardcoded twice.

The same Jellyfin progress-report payload shape ({ItemId, MediaSourceId, PlaySessionId, PositionTicks: Math.round(currentTime*10_000_000)}) is reconstructed in four places with slightly different field sources (currentTime vs videoId capture). This duplication is error-prone; a single buildProgressReport(video, playerProps, seconds) helper would remove the repetition and the repeated ticks conversion.

PUBLIC env var keys (PUBLIC_API_URL, PUBLIC_APP_URL) are hardcoded as bare string literals here and also referenced as a string literal in env-static-public.ts; these are env-key identifiers consumed elsewhere in the app. Not canonical-code drift, but the env-key names are duplicated across the two mock files with no shared constant. Low risk since this is test-only mock surface mirroring framework modules.

Mocks $env/static/public by exporting only PUBLIC_API_URL, while env-dynamic-public.ts also exports PUBLIC_APP_URL. If app code reads PUBLIC_APP_URL from $env/static/public it would resolve undefined silently in tests due to the incomplete static mock. Minor consistency gap between the two public-env mocks.

matchMedia mock always returns matches:false; any test depending on prefers-reduced-motion / responsive breakpoint queries cannot exercise the true branch. Acceptable default but worth noting as a coverage limitation, not a bug.

CollectionRuleCondition.field and CollectionRuleFieldDef.field are free-form strings whose valid values are enumerated only inside the COLLECTION_RULE_FIELDS table (title/rating/date/.../videoSeriesId etc.). These field keys are used for query dispatch on the backend; a string-literal union (or generated code) for the field set would prevent typos and keep the frontend rule builder and backend evaluator aligned.

supportedAnimatedExtensions is folded into supportedGalleryMediaExtensions and treated as image gallery items, while supportedVideoExtensions independently lists overlapping extensions (.mp4/.mkv/.mov/etc.). isImageFile therefore returns true for .mp4/.webm (confirmed by index.test.ts:63-64). The video-vs-gallery-animated extension sets overlap with no single ownership rule, which is an easy classification-ambiguity/drift source as formats are added.

isAnimatedFormat redundantly OR-checks `ext === ".gif"` even though ".gif" is not in supportedAnimatedExtensions but IS in supportedImageExtensions; the special-case is correct but encodes the gif-is-animated rule in code rather than in the extension Sets, so the Sets alone do not describe truth. Minor maintainability smell.

ABSOLUTE_EPISODE_PATTERN requires a trailing separator+space class so a bare absolute episode number at end-of-name (e.g. 'One Piece - 005' with no following token) would not match; combined with the 1900-2099 guard at :78 this is intentional but undocumented edge behavior — worth a comment/test, not a defect.

ParsedEpisodeFilename is declared in types.ts but nothing in this package produces it — there is no parse-episode-filename.ts alongside the movie/season/series parsers. Either dead/aspirational type or a missing parser; worth confirming a consumer exists.

parseMovieFilename strips the extension via EXTENSION_STRIP and then delegates entirely to parseSeriesFolder, discarding any season/episode signal. For movie files this is fine, but the layering means 'movie' and 'series folder' parsing share one cleanup path with no movie-specific tokens (e.g. edition tags); ParsedMovieFilename is effectively a subset projection of ParsedSeriesFolder.

parseSeasonFolder never returns a non-null title except for Specials (always title:null for numeric seasons), yet ParsedSeasonFolder.title is documented as 'Optional display title if the folder name contained one'. The function cannot extract a season display title from names like 'Season 1 - The Beginning', so the title field is mostly vestigial for the numeric path.

RELEASE_TOKEN_PATTERN '-[A-Za-z0-9]+$' (line 26) is not global and strips only the final trailing -GROUP, and YEAR_PATTERN_BARE replace uses the captured year string via working.replace(bareMatch[1]) which replaces the first occurrence of that 4-digit string anywhere — if the title legitimately contains the same 4 digits earlier it could remove the wrong span. Edge-case correctness/robustness risk in the heuristic title cleaner.

CorruptMediaError defines its own 'cause?: Error' field and shadows the standard Error.cause; isCorruptMediaError relies on regex-matching ffprobe/ffmpeg English error message text (line 64-74), which is locale/version-fragile — a brittle but contained heuristic for corruption detection.

toCandidates returns gallery/book candidates with the same shape but the cast `as T` is unchecked — NormalizedGalleryCandidate and NormalizedBookCandidate are structurally identical interfaces (types.ts:284 vs 324), so the generic adds no safety and the two interfaces are duplicate definitions that could be one type.

Doc comment at top says credentials are stored as 'salt:iv:ciphertext' (3 parts) but the implementation and the function-level comment use 'salt:iv:ciphertext:tag' (4 parts, enforced by parts.length !== 4 at line 68). The file-header comment is stale/incorrect.

ScraperAction includes "scrapeJson" and "stash" engine kinds, but the executor/xpath-scraper only handle "script" and "scrapeXPath"; resolveActionDef will happily return a scrapeJson/stash action and scrapeScene will then mis-route it (it falls through to runScraperScript which throws actionDef.action !== "script"). Declared-but-unhandled action kinds with no explicit unsupported-engine error.

TokenBucket.acquire computes waitMs and sleeps but does not re-loop/guard against concurrent acquirers — multiple in-flight acquire() calls can each subtract a token after a single refill, allowing brief over-budget bursts (the rate limiter is best-effort, not strictly serialized). Acceptable for read-only use but worth noting given it guards an external API.

Sprite/background-position math and trickplay-frame indexing are reimplemented inline (lines 123-185, 229-251) and overlap conceptually with the exported helpers in lib/trickplay.ts (findFrameAtTime/timeToTrackPosition). MediaCard rolls its own findIndex + clamp fallback in updateActiveFrame instead of reusing findFrameAtTime, so the two code paths can drift in edge-case behavior.

The icon slug strings here and the keys in apps/web-svelte/src/lib/components/app-shell-nav-icon-map.ts are two halves of one mapping with no shared type binding them; a renamed/typo slug silently yields no icon. appShellNavIconMap also declares extra keys (grid-2x2, layout-list, palette, wrench) not used by any section here, indicating drift between producer and consumer of the slug vocabulary.

MediaCard depends on global utility/component classes that live in the app, not the package: surface-card-sharp, media-card-shell, media-chip, media-chip-accent, pill-accent, tag-chip/tag-chip-default, text-glow-accent, text-ephemeral (lines 191-393). The shared component is not self-contained — it only renders correctly if the consuming app defines these classes (app.css), which weakens the package boundary.

loadTrickplayFrames caches by URL forever in a module-level Map (line 11) with no eviction or error invalidation; a failed/rejected promise is left cached so a transient fetch failure permanently blocks retrying that map URL for the page lifetime.

Dead ternary: `const ledSize = $derived(size === "sm" ? "led-sm" : "led-sm")` returns the same value in both branches, so the size condition is meaningless. Either both LED sizes should differ (e.g. md should not be led-sm) or the ternary should be a plain constant.

Token values (easing curves, durations, keyframe rgba shadows) are duplicated in literal form here and also embedded directly in component Tailwind classes/CSS (e.g. duration-fast, shadow rgba(242,194,106,...) appear independently in TextInput/Toggle and in colors.ts glow). These token consts and the Tailwind theme appear to be parallel sources rather than one feeding the other; drift risk between tokens/*.ts and the actual generated CSS variables. Worth confirming a single generation path from these consts to the Tailwind/CSS-var layer.
